value.Title: Novonix Limited (ASX:NVX) Divests Battery Tech Division for US$1 as It Splits into Three
value.Overview: Australian Company News Bites - Stock Report, 1065words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHH-G7C3-SFSX-M1HS-00000-00Novonix Limited (ASX:NVX) Divests Battery Tech Division for US$1 as It Splits into Three2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisNovonix Limited (ASX:NVX) Divests Battery Tech Division for US$1 as It Splits into Three

AUSTRALIAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

AUSTRALIA EDITION

07 May 2026 08:17 AEST

Novonix Limited (ASX:NVX) reconfigures its business by selling the battery technology division back to its Canadian founder, Chris Burns, for US$1, disclosed on 5th May 2026 at 11:26:01.801688. The company will be divided into three distinct companies, with the publicly traded entity remaining in Chattanooga, Tennessee, and focusing on its synthetic graphite factory.

Burns will oversee the battery technology division from Halifax under two entities, Avrion Battery Labs and Dryve Battery Materials. Avrion Battery Labs will handle research, development and battery testing services, while Dryve Battery Materials will develop manufacturing methods for Li ion battery cathode materials. Novonix retains a 15 percent equity stake in Dryve Battery Materials and transfers US$2 million in cash assets previously owned by the battery technology business.

Investors welcomed the move, and the company noted the restructuring aims to enhance efficiency and position the business for growth in the EV and renewable energy sectors

SECTION 2 NOVONIX ACTIVITIES

Activities

Novonix Limited, an Australian technology company, specializes in the development and supply of advanced materials for the energy storage sector, particularly high-performance synthetic graphite used in lithium-ion batteries. Its core business revolves around innovating anode materials that enhance battery efficiency, longevity, and safety, catering to applications in electric vehicles (EVs), renewable energy storage, and consumer electronics. The company's fastest-growing segments include the production of synthetic graphite for EVs, fueled by the global surge in demand for sustainable transportation, as well as its expanding battery testing and characterization services, which support manufacturers in optimizing product performance. Novonix is strategically positioned to capitalize on the clean energy transition, with ongoing investments in research and production capacity to meet rising market needs, solidifying its role as a key innovator in the battery materials industry. It is Australia's 130th largest Materials company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: Matthew McGill: "If Sánchez's Falcon flies to the U.S., it could be seized due to Spain 's unpaid debts."

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ7-CWD3-SCKX-900N-00000-00Matthew McGill: "If Sánchez's Falcon flies to the U.S., it could be seized due to Spain's unpaid debts."2026-05-09T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisMatthew McGill: "If Sánchez's Falcon flies to the U.S., it could be seized due to Spain's unpaid debts."


Matthew McGill, the attorney representing the majority of companies affected by the government's failure to pay for renewable energy, told Libertad Digital that the compensatory measures sought by these companies to recoup their losses could impact the use of the official Falcon jet typically used by the Royal Household and the Spanish government for official travel.


The partner at the law firm King & Spalding visited Madrid to explain the legal strategy being pursued by the companies hit by the non-payments from Pedro Sánchez's government, which owes more than 2.31 billion euros to 27 investors who suffered the retroactive withdrawal of renewable energy subsidies.
Seven legal victories in the U.S.
During his visit to the Spanish capital, he noted: "The U.S. courts have ruled in our favor in seven cases, and if the petition for protection that Spain has filed with the Supreme Court is not granted, there will be no other recourse to prevent a wide range of seizures of sovereign assets -which, obviously, could be avoided if the court rulings were simply complied with and the breaches were brought to an end."
In this regard, Matthew McGill explained: "For example, if the Falcon jet used by the government or the Royal Household is used to travel to the U.S. in the near future-perhaps to attend a match by the Spanish national team at the World Cup- then we can seek its seizure and confiscation."
The World Cup, in the crosshairs
Referring to the sporting competition, the American lawyer explained: "U.S. courts have given the green light for us to take action in all states of the Union, without restriction, and, of course, we have already gotten to work and begun sending the corresponding legal notices. Spain is going to hold its training camp in Tennessee, is going to play in Atlanta... and we are already preparing other similar actions."
Matthew McGill also noted: "The courts in the United Kingdom, Australia, Belgium, the Netherlands, and Singapore have also ruled in favor of those affected, so the seizures we've seen in recent weeks are just the tip of the iceberg of what's to come if Spain continues to engage in this type of behavior."

value.Title: 2026 World Cup: Which national team could have its prize money seized due to the country's outstanding debts?

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JK8-RSJ3-SBCR-H4PJ-00000-002026 World Cup: Which national team could have its prize money seized due to the country's outstanding debts?2026-05-14T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexis2026 World Cup: Which national team could have its prize money seized due to the country's outstanding debts?


Spain's participation in the 2026 World Cup, which kicks off on June 11 at Azteca Stadium in Mexico City, faces an unexpected risk: potential asset seizures stemming from the country's ongoing litigation with international funds and companies over retroactive cuts to renewable energy subsidies.
The situation is worsening as the World Cup approaches because much of the Spanish national team's activity will take place on U.S. soil, increasing the likelihood that creditors will attempt to enforce seizures by taking advantage of favorable jurisdiction.
International creditors are attempting to collect debts from the Spanish government, which has refused to pay the compensation ordered in recent years by various national and international courts due to the retroactive change in law through which Spaincut investment benefits for renewable energy projects in 2013 .
A few months ago, a Washington court authorized the Blasket Renewable Investments fund-which now owns several cases related to renewable energy claims- to seize all types of deposits and assets held by Spainin the United States in order to collect a debt ofmore than 90 millioneuros. This is the amount of compensation that the World Bank's International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes (ICSID) ordered Spain to pay in the Watkins case in 2021.


Five years later, the Spanish national team will play in the World Cup in the United States, and this presents a prime opportunity for creditors to collect the debt.
According to sources close to the international creditors, any financial transaction linked to Spain's participation in the tournament could be subject to monitoring and eventual seizure.
"We are going to monitor every detail of the operation," they warn, referring to the seven judgments pending payment that they have obtained in various U.S. jurisdictions.
In light of this non-payment, a federal court in Washington ordered FIFA to hand over all information requested by the court. Specifically, "any document or communication under the company's control related to tournament organization services, including logistics planning, fund management, contracts, and operational coordination in the United States," according to the aforementioned media outlet.
The documents requested from FIFA cover the period from January 1, 2024, to the present. Among the documents requested are all agreements through which the soccer governing body has provided services related to Spain records of payments made to or from Spanish entities, including bank transfers, SWIFT, ACH, or Fedwire messages, and account statements; and all communications between FIFA and Spanish entities related to its role as a tournament organizer.
Therefore, when the Spanish National Team arrives in the United States-where its base during the first phase of the World Cup will be located in Chattanooga, Tennessee-all the funds it needs for its day-to-day operations during the tournament could be subject to seizure.
When referring to the term "Spain," the subpoena covers the Spanish government, the RFEF, the Higher Sports Council (CSD), the Ministry of Education, Vocational Training, and Sports, and the Spanish national team itself. It also includes other state entities such as the Bank of Spain, SEPI, the Cervantes Institute, Renfe, and Paradores.
Sources from the defense team for the Blasket Renewable Investments fund indicate that legal summonses have already begun to be sent to suppliers and sponsors of the Spanish team. Among them are Adidas, the official sponsor, and Hilton, the hotel chain where the Spanish players will be staying during their participation in the World Cup, which begins on June 14. Creditors are also targeting the Baylor School training center.
With the competition schedule in hand, they have also tracked down other "commercial clients of the National Team"-service providers the team might use in the various U.S. states where they will play if they advance through the tournament.
There was even mention of the possibility that, should Spain win the World Cup, it might have to use the $50 million ((EURO)42.7 million) prize money to settle its debt with the Blasket Renewable fund.
They also point to a possible seizure of the Dassault Falcon 900 or another official aircraft should the king or the prime minister travel on it to the United States to attend a match.
However, there is more at stake than soccer. The funds also point to the $1.44 billion contract with the U.S. company Raytheon to purchase four Patriot missile defense systems. Spain has appealed to the U.S. Supreme Court, but while the case is pending, the seizures are proceeding.
Meanwhile, sources within the Spanish government assure that this legal situation will not affect Spain's operations, much less those of the Royal Spanish Football Federation (RFEF), through the Spanish national team, as it is an entity independent of the state.

value.Title: TVA SIGNS AGREEMENT WITH TENASKA AFFILIATE FOR 225-MW STORAGE PROJECT

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJX-8CS3-RV5X-Y33R-00000-00TVA SIGNS AGREEMENT WITH TENASKA AFFILIATE FOR 225-MW STORAGE PROJECT2026-05-11T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T15:58:42ZLexisNexisTVA SIGNS AGREEMENT WITH TENASKA AFFILIATE FOR 225-MW STORAGE PROJECTStates News ServiceWASHINGTON

The following information was released by the American Public Power Association:

Bobwhite Energy Storage, an affiliate of independent energy company Tenaska, has signed a long-term energy storage agreement with Tennessee Valley Authority for a 225-megawatt/900-megawatt hour storage project in East Tennessee.

The Bobwhite project is expected to start construction in 2027 and come online in late 2029.

The agreement with TVA spans 20 years "and supports TVA's strategic commitment to strengthen grid reliability, manage peak demand and integrate more resources to help keep power affordable for hardworking American families.

"Battery storage is essential to protecting the reliable, affordable electricity our region depends on to power next-generation technologies," said Monika Beckner, TVA Vice President, Power Supply and Fuels. "Projects like Bobwhite strengthen the Valley's energy security, increase our ability to manage extreme conditions and help unleash American energy building a resilient system that supports families, drives economic growth and reinforces America's energy dominance."

With this agreement, TVA has signed 425 megawatts of standalone battery energy storage systems to support capacity needs throughout its seven-state service territory.

Tenaska, based in Omaha, Nebraska, has successfully developed approximately 14,000 megawatts of natural gas-fueled and renewable power projects. The company's development portfolio includes more than 33,000 megawatts of energy projects, including 7,000 megawatts of battery storage.

Construction and operation of the Bobwhite project is expected to boost the local economy, with a total estimated investment of approximately $300 million.

The project will create up to 75 jobs during peak construction, "prioritizing skilled workers from the surrounding communities and supporting TVA's commitment to investing in the people who call the Valley home," Tenaska noted.

"In addition to new job opportunities, the project will create meaningful avenues for local businesses to provide goods and services, helping to keep economic benefits rooted in the community and supporting long-term regional growth," Tenaska said.

value.Title: Korea Zinc posts record Q1 earnings on strong demand for critical minerals

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-CVM3-RV7P-72F1-00000-00Korea Zinc posts record Q1 earnings on strong demand for critical minerals 2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisKorea Zinc posts record Q1 earnings on strong demand for critical minerals

Korea Zinc reported record-breaking earnings for the first quarter this year, primarily driven by a surge in demand for critical minerals.

According to the company on Wednesday, it posted 6.07 trillion won ($4.19 billion) in revenue and 746.1 billion won in operating profit, up 58.4 percent and 175.2 percent year-on-year, respectively. This extended its streak of record operating profit to 105 consecutive quarters.

Operating margin rose by 5.2 percentage points to 12.3 percent in the same period, reflecting a significant improvement in profitability.

The company said its strong performance is notable given rising global uncertainties, including geopolitical tensions in the Middle East and disruptions to key supply routes such as the Strait of Hormuz.

Leveraging its diversified product portfolio and stable production capabilities, Korea Zinc saw strong demand for critical minerals such as antimony ? used in advanced and defense industries ? alongside increased demand for precious metals like gold and silver as investors shift capital into lower-risk assets.

Another key contributor was the company's Troika Drive strategy, introduced after Chairman Choi Yun-birm took office in 2022. The initiative ? focused on renewable energy and green hydrogen, battery materials and resource recycling ? began generating meaningful profits last year. Notably, growth in copper sales and the expansion of its recycling subsidiary, PedalPoint, further bolstered earnings.

In line with its shareholder return policy, Korea Zinc declared a quarterly dividend of 5,000 won per share, with a total payout of 102 billion won. The company has maintained a shareholder return target of 40 percent from 2024 to 2026 and has consistently exceeded this benchmark. It also demonstrated its commitment by canceling 2.04 million treasury shares last year.

The company reaffirmed its commitment to long-term growth, highlighting Project Crucible, a $7.4 billion plan to build an integrated smelter in Tennessee. The project, designated under the US government's FAST-41 program, is set to benefit from expedited permitting and strong federal and state support, positioning it as a key hub for critical minerals and supply chain resilience.

value.Title: Novonix Limited (ASX:NVX) Reconfigures Business Structure, Sells Battery Tech Division for US$1
value.Overview: Australian Company News Bites - Stock Report, 1078words

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHC-G413-RT9J-20V8-00000-00Novonix Limited (ASX:NVX) Reconfigures Business Structure, Sells Battery Tech Division for US$12026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisNovonix Limited (ASX:NVX) Reconfigures Business Structure, Sells Battery Tech Division for US$1

AUSTRALIAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

AUSTRALIA EDITION

06 May 2026 20:18 AEST

Novonix Limited (ASX:NVX) reconfigures its business structure by selling its battery technology division back to its Canadian founder, Chris Burns, for US$1. The transaction, disclosed on 5th May 2026 at 11:26:01.801688, splits the company into three distinct entities, with the publicly traded unit based in Chattanooga, Tennessee, and focused on its synthetic graphite factory.

Under the new arrangement, Burns will run the battery technology division from Halifax through two entities: Avrion Battery Labs, which will handle research and development and battery testing services, and Dryve Battery Materials, which will develop manufacturing methods for lithium ion battery cathode materials. Novonix will retain a 15 percent equity stake in Dryve Battery Materials and part with US$2 million in cash assets previously owned by the battery technology business.

The restructuring aims to sharpen operational efficiency and capitalize on demand for advanced battery technologies. Investors welcomed the news, with Novonix shares rising eight percent on the ASX. The split enables Burns to advance battery technologies in Canada while preserving a stake in the new Dryve venture, potentially benefiting the broader battery ecosystem as electric vehicle and renewable energy markets expand

SECTION 2 NOVONIX ACTIVITIES

Activities

Novonix Limited, an Australian technology company, specializes in the development and supply of advanced materials for the energy storage sector, particularly high-performance synthetic graphite used in lithium-ion batteries. Its core business revolves around innovating anode materials that enhance battery efficiency, longevity, and safety, catering to applications in electric vehicles (EVs), renewable energy storage, and consumer electronics. The company's fastest-growing segments include the production of synthetic graphite for EVs, fueled by the global surge in demand for sustainable transportation, as well as its expanding battery testing and characterization services, which support manufacturers in optimizing product performance. Novonix is strategically positioned to capitalize on the clean energy transition, with ongoing investments in research and production capacity to meet rising market needs, solidifying its role as a key innovator in the battery materials industry. It is Australia's 130th largest Materials company by market capitalisation.

urn:contentItem:6JK1-KGG3-RRS4-X32T-00000-0020 Years After 'An Inconvenient Truth,' Al Gore Grapples With the (Big) Wrinkle of AI2026-05-13T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexis20 Years After 'An Inconvenient Truth,' Al Gore Grapples With the (Big) Wrinkle of AI

NASHVILLE, Tenn.-Former Vice President Al Gore is knocking on wood.

Seated in front of portraits of Johnny Cash and Prince inside The Pinnacle, a Nashville music venue, Gore told Inside Climate News that despite the current state of both national and global politics, he believes humanity can and will surmount the challenge of human-induced climate change. 

But that's not the whole story, he said. 

To meet the challenge presented by a rapidly warming world, Gore said it will take more than cheap talk. It will take action. 

Just before sitting down for a conversation at the beginning of May, Gore presented his climate change slideshow, its delivery perfected over two decades, to a room of hundreds of members of the Climate Reality Project, a Gore-led nonprofit that focuses on recruiting, training and mobilizing people to help speed the transition to a clean energy future. 

Now a network of over 4.5 million people globally, Gore said the nonprofit's trainees will be pivotal in moving forward the work of ending fossil fuel dependency and pushing toward a just transition to renewable energy.

Given the white-knuckled grasp of fossil fuel funders over public officials, he said, that transition won't happen without a struggle and won't happen overnight. Instead, it will take the commitment of Americans of all color, class and creed to secure the policy changes needed to ensure a stable future. 

Among the many challenges facing those concerned with curbing carbon emissions is the exponential explosion in planned energy generation and consumption associated with the AI boom. Across the country, communities have come together to oppose the power- and water-hungry data centers that power AI technology as the nation's tech behemoths dash to secure their place in the race for AI supremacy. 

In Alabama, for example, residents have almost universally opposed a data center development called Project Marvel in Bessemer, just outside Birmingham, that would lead to the clearcutting of hundreds of acres of agricultural land and, at full buildout, consume 1,200 megawatts of energy annually-enough electricity to power a million homes. That single development alone, just one of many data center proposals across Alabama, is slated to increase Alabama Power's demand for energy statewide by as much as 10 percent. 

Developments like Project Marvel are worrying, Gore acknowledged. 

"I read your reports," Gore said of Inside Climate News' reporting from Bessemer. "I think it is a real problem. I think that it's a problem on a national and global scale that is a cause for deep concern, but not panic."

Gore, now 78, said he isn't panicking for a few reasons. First, he said, while utilities in some regions of the U.S. may choose to fuel the data center boom with dirty energy, including Alabama Power, other utilities and some large tech companies themselves have committed to adopting renewables like wind and solar over fossil fuels. In 2025, giant tech companies like Google, Amazon, Microsoft and Meta accounted for about half of clean power purchase agreements globally, according to a report by BloombergNEF, though the report's authors also noted that corporate demand for clean energy had fallen 10 percent year over year, the first such decline in nearly a decade. 

While Gore said considering ways to reduce overall energy consumption is reasonable, it may be difficult to curb the surge in electricity demand fueled by AI data centers. 

"Yes, the underlying problem is in part that we are just continuing to use so much energy," Gore said. "But with this AI revolution, which is unlike any scientific revolution in prior history, it is going to be a real challenge to try and hold that back."

The construction of AI data centers across the country may be inevitable, Gore suggested, so the goal should be to encourage tech developers to co-locate renewables at data center sites. Gore said he prefers incentivizing the colocation of clean energy production to other strategies, including that of Maine's legislature, which recently passed a moratorium on new data centers in the state that was later vetoed by Gov. Janet Mills. 

And while the demand for energy spurred by AI has already led to significant fossil fuel investment, Gore said he's hopeful that AI tech can be deployed in specific ways to actually reduce carbon emissions.

Gore pointed to a recent study by researchers at the London School of Economics that concluded widespread adoption of AI tools as a means of reducing emissions could be transformative, potentially outweighing AI's energy costs. The study considered AI tools that could increase efficiencies in areas like grid management, food production and transportation and found that "the emissions reductions due to AI from just these three sectors alone would more than offset the estimated increase in emissions from all of AI's activities." 

Nicholas Stern, chair of the Grantham Research Institute and the paper's lead author, has warned, however, that the widespread adoption of AI as an emissions reduction tool is not inevitable. Without appropriate public policy, the energy costs of AI and resulting emissions are unlikely to be mitigated by the technology's emissions reductions potential. 

"Governments have a critical role in ensuring that AI is deployed effectively to accelerate the transition equitably and sustainably," Stern said. "Without active public policy, the commercial incentives to apply AI in the socially productive areas described may be weak."

A 2025 report by the International Energy Agency (IEA) was more explicitly skeptical: 

"It is vital to note that there is currently no momentum that could ensure the widespread adoption of these AI applications," the report concluded, referring to the use of renewable energy to power data centers. "Therefore, their aggregate impact, even in 2035, could be marginal if the necessary enabling conditions are not created."

Gore said he also believes that data center developers may find themselves without the public support necessary for such rapid growth. Recent polling suggests that significantly more Americans say they would oppose the construction of a data center in their neighborhood than say they would support it.

But in places like Alabama, near-universal opposition to data center developments has done little to slow the data center boom. Instead, public officials have glossed over residents' concerns, often pointing to the promise of tax revenue and job creation as paramount. 

Gore said he feels for residents in places like Bessemer who feel their concerns are being entirely dismissed. 

"I'm on their side," he said. "I understand their frustration and anger."

Ultimately, unresponsive elected officials are a result of an increasingly ineffective system, according to Gore-a system centered around pleasing donors, not representing the interests of the average American. 

"We have seen a capture of governmental processes in many areas with money playing way too big a role in the political process," he said. 

Still, Gore said he believes the writing is on the wall when it comes to public sentiment toward the way the Trump administration and many states are handling environmental issues. Since Trump's election, Gore said Americans have begun to better understand the consequences of the president's public policy preferences. 

"This president campaigned on 'No more wars in the Middle East. No more rising prices. We're going to bring inflation down,'" Gore said. "All the things he promised, he's doing the exact opposite."

Gore pointed to Trump's reported April 2024 meeting with oil executives where he asked for $1 billion in campaign funding. 

"He said 'Give me a billion dollars and I will do whatever you want-I'll get rid of regulations and laws that are not convenient for you,'" Gore said of Trump. "And they gave him the billion dollars, and we're seeing the transaction completed now."

Gore spoke faster and louder as he discussed the president's agenda. 

"I'm sorry," he said. "You triggered me there."

Americans aren't naive, Gore said. Come November, he believes Republicans may see the electoral chickens come home to roost. Gore rapped his knuckles on the table in front of him.

"Knock on wood," he said. 

Just two days before ICN's conversation with Gore, the U.S. Supreme Court's decision in Louisiana v. Callais had ignited a rush to eliminate majority-minority districts across the South. The day of the interview, state legislatures in Alabama and Tennessee were called into special sessions to solidify any possible advantage for the GOP in November's midterm elections. 

The last-minute election map changes across the country spurred by the high court's intervention may become an obstacle to adopting sound climate policy. 

"I think it's a problem for democracy," Gore said. "And in order to solve the climate crisis, we're going to have to address the democracy crisis. This is a move very much in the wrong direction where democracy is concerned."

Before the end of the conversation, Gore touched on America's lurching role in solving the climate crisis. Unfortunately, he said, the Trump administration has eroded our ability to lead on issues of climate. In some ways, the dynamic has left China with more international credibility on the issue than the United States, according to Gore. 

"That's because the United States is led by someone who is profoundly dishonest and is the most corrupt president in all of American history," Gore said. "Case closed."

While China's continued investment in coal is a "demerit" for the country, Gore explained, analysts expect that the Chinese government will continue to lead the world in the buildout of solar and wind capacity. 

"Once they [shift away from coal], most expectations are that China's emissions are going to decline quite dramatically for a long period of time," he said. 

Whether in China or the United States, Gore said that solving the climate crisis remains a challenge but that we have the available technology to get the job done. The barrier remains in the political sphere, where the political influence of the fossil fuel industry continues to put a stranglehold on efforts to quicken a just energy transition. 

That barrier is evident now more than ever in the South, Gore said, where legislative majorities are sometimes effectively extensions of the fossil fuel lobby itself. 

"The fossil fuel industry is in league with the Trump administration and the governments of a number of southern states in particular," Gore said. "And they have so much power that they just keep politicians under their thumb and tell them what to do, and they say, 'Yes, sir,' and do it."

But those interested in the future of American democracy-and, for that matter, life on Earth-failure is not an option, Gore said. 

"I have 10 grandchildren, but no greats yet," Gore said, a smile curling on his face. "We have the opportunity to shape the future. I would prefer to shape it into a clean, egalitarian future for everyone."

Damage-some irreversible-has already been done to the Earth's climate, Gore said, and worse is yet to come. But the most dire changes aren't inevitable, either. 

"We can still prevent the most catastrophic changes that are feared," Gore said. "But right now we're running a reckless risk of crossing the tipping point that would make the future a worse time for our grandchildren. Let's change that."

urn:contentItem:6JHB-GJ53-RS0W-S133-00000-00Novonix Reconfigures Business Structure, Sells Battery Tech Division for US$12026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisNovonix Reconfigures Business Structure, Sells Battery Tech Division for US$1

AUSTRALIAN DAILY STOCK REPORT

AUSTRALIA EDITION

06 May 2026 14:17 AEST

Novonix Limited has announced a significant restructuring of its business, selling its battery technology divisions back to its Canadian founder, Chris Burns, for a nominal price of US$1. This move, which is part of a broader strategy to streamline operations, was disclosed on 5th May 2026 at 11:26:01.801688. As a result of the transaction, Novonix will be divided into three distinct companies, with the publicly-traded entity remaining based in Chattanooga, Tennessee, and focusing on its synthetic graphite factory.

The transaction marks a notable homecoming for a substantial Canadian technology venture. Both Burns and Jeff Dahn, a renowned battery researcher, gained prominence for their vital contributions to battery technology at Tesla. Under the new arrangement, Novonix will retain a 15 percent equity stake in the newly formed Dryve Battery Materials, along with parting with US$2 million in cash assets that were previously owned by the battery technology business. Burns is set to run the battery technology division from Halifax under two separate companies: Avrion Battery Labs and Dryve Battery Materials.

Avrion Battery Labs will concentrate on research and development, as well as battery testing services, while Dryve Battery Materials will focus on developing manufacturing methods for lithium-ion battery cathode materials. This strategic pivot allows Novonix to hone in on its core competencies while enabling Burns to leverage his expertise in advancing battery technologies in Canada.

Investors reacted positively to the news, with Novonix shares rising by eight percent on the Australian Securities Exchange following the announcement. This uptick reflects market confidence in the company's renewed focus on its synthetic graphite operations and the potential growth of its stake in Dryve. The restructuring aims to enhance operational efficiency and capitalize on the growing demand for innovative battery technologies.

As the electric vehicle and renewable energy sectors continue to expand, Novonix's refocused strategy may position it favorably within the competitive landscape. The split allows both Novonix and the newly established entities under Burns to pursue their respective goals more effectively, potentially leading to advancements in battery technology that could benefit the entire industry.

SECTION 2 NOVONIX ACTIVITIES

Activities

Novonix Limited, an Australian technology company, specializes in the development and supply of advanced materials for the energy storage sector, particularly high-performance synthetic graphite used in lithium-ion batteries. Its core business revolves around innovating anode materials that enhance battery efficiency, longevity, and safety, catering to applications in electric vehicles (EVs), renewable energy storage, and consumer electronics. The company's fastest-growing segments include the production of synthetic graphite for EVs, fueled by the global surge in demand for sustainable transportation, as well as its expanding battery testing and characterization services, which support manufacturers in optimizing product performance. Novonix is strategically positioned to capitalize on the clean energy transition, with ongoing investments in research and production capacity to meet rising market needs, solidifying its role as a key innovator in the battery materials industry. It is Australia's 129th largest Materials company by market capitalisation.

value.Title: Skyview Ventures Expands Biodiversity And Data-Driven Beekeeping Program Across Multi-State Solar Portfolio With Hivetracks

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-PDH3-RRM5-K2W8-00000-00Skyview Ventures Expands Biodiversity And Data-Driven Beekeeping Program Across Multi-State Solar Portfolio With Hivetracks2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisSkyview Ventures Expands Biodiversity And Data-Driven Beekeeping Program Across Multi-State Solar Portfolio With Hivetracks

Innovative initiative integrates pollinator habitat, biodiversity monitoring, and environmental data collection across solar projects
NASHVILLE, Tenn. and CRESTON, N.C., May 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ -- Skyview Ventures, a U.S.-based renewable energy investor, together with its solar company, Davis Hill Development, today announces the expansion of its partnership with HiveTracks, a biodiversity intelligence platform, to scale data-driven environmental monitoring and managed pollinator programs across its solar portfolio. The program integrates apiaries, environmental monitoring, and pollinator habitats into operating and developing solar projects across multiple U.S. states.


Continue Reading
Skyview Ventures is both an investor in HiveTracks and the company's first solar industry client. The collaboration began in 2025 with eight solar projects across New York, West Virginia, Tennessee, and Connecticut. Following the success of the initial rollout, Skyview Ventures plans to expand the program to additional sites in 2026.
"This partnership reflects our broader vision that renewable energy projects should be developed with long-term environmental stewardship in mind," said Andy Karetsky, CEO of Skyview Ventures. "By integrating biodiversity monitoring, pollinator habitats, and data-driven land management into our solar portfolio, we are demonstrating that solar projects can deliver environmental benefits beyond clean energy generation and play a meaningful role in supporting local ecosystems."
Each participating solar site is managed by local beekeepers recruited and supported by HiveTracks. These apiaries serve not only for honey production but also as environmental monitoring tools, offering insight into ecosystem health and land use impact over time
HiveTracks produces environmental metrics, visualized on a per site and portfolio level, generating actionable recommendations to improve ecological conditions and biodiversity. The data collected comprise pollinator and plant diversity and abundance, and are complemented with hive-based testing for eDNA and ecotoxicology. In 2025, the honey produced at the program's apiaries was bottled and donated to food banks and given away as gifts.
The program spans projects at multiple stages of development-from pre-construction through operations-allowing Skyview Ventures to evaluate environmental impact across the full lifecycle of solar project development. Following annual analysis, HiveTracks provides site-specific recommendations that allow Davis Hill Development, the project operator, to implement pollinator-friendly operations and maintenance practices, and improve habitat conditions over time.
"Solar energy already provides environmental benefits, but with HiveTracks, we saw an opportunity to go further," said Maria Morales Ferrebus, Solar Development Associate at Skyview Ventures. "In our first year, the data provided valuable insights into ecosystem health across our sites. For example, we identified areas with low plant diversity, which can reduce resilience to drought conditions and increase erosion risk. By implementing HiveTracks' recommendations, we can adapt our operations and maintenance practices to enhance plant diversity, improve soil health, and strengthen pollinator habitats."
Together, these site-level insights are informing how Skyview Ventures approaches land stewardship across its portfolio. By integrating apiaries and environmental monitoring into its solar projects, the company is creating a scalable model that supports pollinator health, enables community agriculture, and generates consistent, site-specific data to guide long-term land management decisions.
"Solar sites represent a unique opportunity to support pollinators and monitor biodiversity at scale," said Max Rünzel, CEO of HiveTracks. "Our partnership with Skyview Ventures demonstrates how beekeeping, environmental data, and renewable energy infrastructure can work together to create measurable environmental impact."
The expanded program reflects a broader trend in the renewable energy industry toward multi-use land strategies, where solar development is combined with agriculture, habitat restoration, and biodiversity initiatives.
About Skyview Ventures
Skyview Ventures has three operating businesses: Skyview Finance Company, one of the largest merchants of Environmental Attributes; Davis Hill Development, which develops, owns, and operates solar and battery storage facilities; and Skycharger, which develops, owns, and operates electric vehicle charging infrastructure. In addition, Skyview Ventures has over 30 venture investments on their portfolio.
The company owns 70 MW of solar assets across 15 states and over 600 EV charging stations. Headquartered in Nashville, TN, with additional offices in Greenwich, CT and Sacramento, CA, Skyview Ventures is a national leader in clean energy.
To learn more, visit .
About HiveTracks
HiveTracks is building the data infrastructure for biodiversity, enabling landowners, developers, and farmers to measure and improve ecological outcomes directly on-site. HiveTracks brings environmental monitoring and sustainable agriculture to the green energy sector by leveraging beehives as biosensors and empowering beekeepers as field ecologists. To learn more, visit .
About Davis Hill Development
Davis Hill Development (DHD), a subsidiary of Skyview Ventures, is a leading developer and owner of distributed solar projects with more than 180 operating assets across 15 states. Founded in 2013, DHD delivers turnkey clean energy solutions for businesses, municipalities, and landowners, with a growing pipeline of community and commercial solar projects. The company specializes in structuring projects that generate long-term value-reducing energy costs, providing lease revenue, and supporting energy equity through community solar access for low-to-moderate income (LMI) residents. Through its parent company, DHD also offers REC and carbon credit solutions to help organizations meet sustainability goals. Learn more at

urn:contentItem:6JH0-2BW3-SH2K-D1J3-00000-00Weyerhaeuser plans new construction project in Gallatin, Tennessee2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisCONSTRUCTION NEWS & PACKAGING AND PAPER NEWSWeyerhaeuser plans new construction project in Gallatin, TennesseeCraig Etkin

Gallatin, Tennessee - According to state and local development sources, Weyerhaeuser plans to invest $10 Million to build out 96,250 square feet of new space in Gallatin. The company is creating 20 new jobs at this facility. The company plans to occupy the new space at 615 Rappahannock Wire Road in Gallatin, on or about December 1, 2026. According to the company website For more than a century, weve been growing trees and making forest products that improve lives in fundamental ways. We manage our millions of acres of forests on a continuous and fully sustainable cycle. We are responsible stewards of our land for multiple uses, including recreation, conservation, economic development and different forms of renewable energy. And the wood products we make are used to build homes where families are sheltered and raised. Weyerhaeuser Company began more than 100 years ago with 900,000 acres of timberland, three employees and a small office in Tacoma, Washington. Founded in 1900 by Frederick Weyerhaeuser, weve grown to become one of the largest sustainable forest products companies in the world. In January 1900, Frederick Weyerhaeuser and 15 partners took a step that stirred the imagination and signaled a new era in the history of the lumber industry. In the largest private land transaction in American history to that time, they purchased 900,000 acres of Washington state timberland from the Northern Pacific Railway.

To learn more about Weyerhaeuser, visit http://www.weyerhaeuser.com/

Company Contact:

Seattle, WA98104
Phone: 206-539-3000

value.Title: PILOT COMPANY - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JHR-SDG3-RRVD-714Y-00000-00PILOT COMPANY - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP2026-05-07T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILOT COMPANY - RECENT INDUSTRY AND PEER NEWS ROUNDUP

DASHBOARD: PILOT COMPANY

Websitehttps://www.pilotcompany.com/IndustryTravel Stops & ConvenienceAddress5508 Lonas Dr, Knoxville, Tennessee 37909, USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pilot Company, founded in 1958 by James Haslam II, is a leading North American travel center operator headquartered in Knoxville, Tennessee. Employing over 30,000 people, it operates 800+ Pilot and Flying J locations across 44 states and Canada, generating $30 billion annually. Providing fuel, food, and truck services, it serves 1.3 million customers daily. In 2024, Pilot expanded its EV charging network and partnered with GM for sustainability initiatives. Its culture emphasizes hospitality, safety, and community, with training in customer service and logistics. Competing with Loves Travel Stops, Pilot stands out for its scale and Berkshire Hathaways 80% ownership (Haslam family retains 20%). Recent developments include digital loyalty apps and $1 billion in store remodels, aligning with travel and eco-trends. Pilots charitable arm supports veterans and education, reinforcing its community focus.

+ PILOT COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ FLYING J REMODEL IN SOUTH BELOIT SPARKS $10,000 EDUCATION DONATIONS

On Mar. 9, 2026, Pilot Company unveiled a remodeled Flying J travel center in South Beloit and pledged $10,000 to local schools under its Miles Good program.The donations will support Hononegah's Stateline robotics program and upgrades to Prairie Hill's playground facilities.The renovation at 16049 Willowbrook Road features an expanded kitchen, a new deli area, upgraded laundry facilities, and self-checkout options, reinforcing the company's commitment to quality service and community investment.

+ PILOT EATS INTRODUCES DUAL-BRAND STRATEGY TO ELEVATE TRAVEL CENTER MEALS

Pilot Company has rolled out Pilot Eats, a new proprietary brand to expand freshly prepared meals at its North American travel centers, with the rollout starting 6th March 2026. The two concepts—Pilot Eats at about 400 centers and Pilot Eats Express at about 200—will be supported by a 24-hour operation at nearly 350 owned-and-operated quick-service locations. Pilot Eats will feature items such as a Southern chicken sandwich with a bigger crispy fillet and Mega Cheese pepperoni pizza by the slice or whole pie, while Express will emphasize grab-and-go options. The company plans a phased 2026 rollout, starting with redesigned stores in Chicopee, Mass., and Ponce de Leon, Fla., and will update packaging and signage in the spring. The Pilot Flying J App will integrate both menus, enabling mobile ordering and loyalty rewards. Promotional deals include a $6 breakfast combo and an $8 pizza-focused lunch or dinner.

+ PILOT COMPANY ACCELERATES EV INFRASTRUCTURE AS NETWORK EXPANDS

Feb. 3 Southern Tire Mart at Pilot now offers maintenance services at more than 130 locations nationwide, including 44 new sites. The EV charging network has surpassed 1,000 charging stalls at about 250 locations, with 27 new sites added since October, as Pilot advances its low carbon fueling and renewable energy initiatives. Founded in 1958 and based in Knoxville, Pilot Company operates the largest travel center network in North America with over 900 locations in 44 states and five Canadian provinces, serving roughly 1.2 million guests daily as a Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QUIKTRIP [3 News That Matters]

+ TEXAS POWERBALL GRAND PRIZE HIT AT LEANDER QUIKTRIP

A QuikTrip in Leander, Texas, sold a Powerball ticket that won a share of a $20 million prize in the May 2, 2026 drawing. The ticket matched all five numbers plus the Powerball, with the numbers 25-37-42-52-65 and Powerball 14. The Texas portion of the prize is estimated at about $4.5 million before taxes, and the prize will be divided with a Florida winner. Winners must claim within 180 days of the draw, and as of the latest update, the winner has not publicly come forward. The Texas Lottery emphasized the significance of the win for players, retailers, and the state’s beneficiaries, including public education and veterans’ services.

+ QUIKTRIP OPENS MINOOKA TRAVEL CENTER, BOLSTERING I-80 CORRIDOR SERVICE

QuikTrip opened a new travel center in Minooka, Ill., along Interstate 80 at Exit 122, on 4th May 2026, expanding its Illinois network.The Minooka center offers a larger lot and a spacious layout, with capacity for 20 gasoline vehicles and six diesel bays for trucks, plus QT Kitchens with grab-and-go meals, made-to-order options, and a 12-beverage coffee program featuring hot and iced lattes made with real milk and freshly ground beans.Aisha Jefferson, QuikTrip's Corporate Communications Manager, said the company is excited about its Minooka entry, noting that cities and towns across Chicagoland and Illinois have embraced QT as more than a convenience store. QT now operates 20 Illinois locations and has stores under construction in Bridgeview and South Holland, while donors contribute 5% of annual net profits to causes in public safety and at-risk youth. Founded in 1958, QT has grown to about $11 billion in revenue with more than 1,200 stores across 22 states.

+ QUIKTRIP ROLLS OUT GEN 4 DESIGN WITH LIGHT, ACCESSIBILITY AND KITCHEN INNOVATION

Apr. 21, 2026 — QuikTrip unveiled its Gen 4 store concept in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, marking its most significant store update since 2010. The 6,300-square-foot Gen 4 sits between the brand's traditional layout and the travel-center model, offering a balanced, modern shopping space. Key features include enhanced storefront glass, a glass vestibule for natural light and better sightlines, and a streamlined checkout layout with eight family-friendly stalls, ADA-compliant facilities and baby-changing stations. Developed over nearly three years with more than 750 discussions, Gen 4 will roll out to future stores in Joplin, MO; Wichita, KS; and Tulsa, OK, with each new location expected to create about two dozen jobs.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SHEETZ [3 News That Matters]

+ SHEETZ GAINS APPROVAL FOR APP-BASED DRIVE-THRU PICKUP AND GAS STATION AT GATEWAY SITE

The Bath Board of Zoning Appeals approved Sheetz's conditional use permits for a gas station and drive-thru at the Ghent Road/I-77 interchange during a session held on 30th April 2026. The drive-thru will function as a pickup window for mobile orders via the Sheetz app, and the site covers 16.11 acres with 3.22 acres for the store; the rest sits within the I-77 right-of-way with potential for future development. Variances for a 40-foot driveway and a 6,139-square-foot building footprint were discussed, with traffic concerns raised by some residents; the board deferred final action until traffic studies by the county engineer and ODOT are completed, while recognizing mixed opinions on the project.

+ PENNSYLVANIA CONVENIENCE CHAIN SLASHES ENERGY USE BY 20% SINCE 2019

Sheetz, the Pennsylvania-based convenience-store retailer, has reduced its energy consumption by 20% across more than 830 stores since joining the U.S. Department of Energy’s Better Buildings Challenge, a milestone reported on 5th May 2026.As the program's sole convenience-store participant, Sheetz is among nearly 300 organizations pursuing a 20% reduction over ten years. 'By joining this challenge, we aimed not only to inspire other retailers to adopt more sustainable business practices, but also to hold ourselves accountable for meaningful results,' he said.Since 2011, all new Sheetz stores have LED lighting and low-flow water fixtures, while existing stores have been retrofitted with advanced HVAC systems and energy-efficient lighting. The company has expanded building optimization and controls across its network and earned the 2024 Innovation Award from Lawrence Berkeley National Laboratory for HVAC control.Sheetz also reports strong waste reductions, with over 95% recycling of corrugated cardboard in its stores, and operates EV charging stations at more than 130 locations as part of building an extensive EV network. The company is slated to be recognized at the Better Buildings & Better Plants Summit in Crystal City, Virginia, on May 11-13.

+ SHEETZ EXPANDS MICHIGAN FOOTPRINT WITH TAYLOR STORE SET TO DEBUT

Sheetz, the Altoona-based convenience-store chain, is expanding in Michigan with a Taylor store at 20623 Eureka Road, opening on 8th May 2026. The move fits into a larger growth plan for the region.Another Fraser location at 32971 Utica Road is also planned, and both stores will operate 24/7.Opening-day activities include giveaways and a donation drive for Forgotten Harvest; donors of non-perishable items will receive a complimentary bag between 9 a.m. and 11 a.m. The company aims to add 14 new Michigan locations this year.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WAWA [3 News That Matters]

+ LOCAL PRIDE IN A CUP: WAWA AND GRITTY COLLABORATE ON PHILADELPHIA-THEMED SMOOTHIE

Wawa has announced a collaboration with Philadelphia Flyers mascot Gritty to promote a new Gritty Smoothie — a fruit smoothie launching in Wawa stores across the Philadelphia area in time for the playoffs. The announcement was made on 5th May 2026, highlighting a local-pride initiative.Now available at more than 400 stores in the region, customers can order the smoothie in-store via the touch-screen system or the Wawa App, with options for pickup, curbside service, and delivery. The move underscores Wawa’s focus on convenience and regional relevance.Doug Martin, Wawa’s Chief Marketing Officer, said the partnership celebrates local pride and a product that embodies the spirit of Philadelphia. He added, \"This partnership was all about having fun and giving our customers something uniquely Philadelphia.\" Gritty offered a playful nod, saying, \"We made a smoothie. You’re welcome.\" With more than 1,100 locations across states including Pennsylvania, New Jersey, Delaware, Maryland, Virginia, Florida, North Carolina, Alabama, Georgia, and Washington, D.C., Wawa emphasized its deep community ties as it rolls out the new drink.

+ FIRST WAWA SET TO OPEN IN MIFFLIN COUNTY, PROMISING CONVENIENCE AND COMMUNITY IMPACT

Mifflin County is preparing to welcome its first Wawa this week, with final preparations underway ahead of the grand opening. As of 28th April 2026, the iconic convenience chain is set to establish a new hub for residents and visitors.Located in a strategic spot, the store is expected to enhance daily convenience and broaden Wawa's footprint in central Pennsylvania. Customers can anticipate a menu featuring freshly prepared sandwiches, coffee, and a range of snacks, underscoring the brand's emphasis on quality and service.Local officials see potential economic benefits, including job creation and increased foot traffic that could benefit the surrounding community.The grand opening is expected to invite promotions and activities that celebrate the occasion, while signaling Wawa's ongoing growth in the region and its focus on community engagement and customer satisfaction.

+ WAWA TESTS ULTRA-FAST FLY THRU PICKUPS WHILE EXPANDING TRAVEL CENTERS

Wawa is expanding its convenience-focused strategy with 30-second Fly Thru pickup windows at select sites as part of a plan to add about 95 new stores in 2026. The Fly Thru concept lets customers order via the Wawa app and pick up without leaving their vehicles. Current deployment includes three Midwest stores and two locations in Langhorne, Pa., and New Jersey. CEO Brian Schaller has underscored the potential of the service, while noting that extending Fly Thru to existing stores will require time and coordination with local authorities. In parallel, Wawa is growing a travel-center footprint for truckers with openings in Indiana and Ohio, described by CEO Chris Gheysens as "Wawa on steroids" with larger stores and improved parking. With roughly 1,200 locations and ongoing renovations, the company says these moves align with a broader shift toward speed and mobile ordering. A related note: a viral discussion about Wawa’s hoagies arose after a book reference by author B.K. Borison.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CASEY'S GENERAL STORES [3 News That Matters]

+ CASEY'S GENERAL STORES, INC. (NASDAQ:CASY) ROLLS OUT SEASONAL PRIVATE LABEL SNACKS FOR SUMMER

ANKENY, Iowa — Casey's General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:CASY) is expanding its summer private-label offerings with a limited-edition lineup packaged in red, white, and blue, available May through August. The expansion builds on the Casey's Brand program, which now encompasses more than 300 items across nearly every in-store category. The lineup features Casey's Brand popcorn, waffle cones, and gummy candy, plus seasonal chips, with a buy one, get one for $1 deal on the snacks and two-for-$5 on chips. Eric Long, vice president of proprietary brands at Casey's, says the seasonal lineup delivers classic summer flavors, variety, and value in packaging that celebrates the season. Casey's operates about 2,900 stores from its Ankeny, Iowa headquarters.

+ CASEY'S GENERAL STORES, INC. (NASDAQ:CASY) BROADENS USE OF SOUNDHOUND VOICE AI ACROSS ITS NETWORK

Casey's General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:CASY) expands its partnership with SoundHound AI Inc. to deploy voice ordering agents across more than 2,600 locations, effective 29th April 2026. The move follows SoundHound's role in handling over 21 million guest interactions to date and aims to enhance guest service and store efficiency during peak ordering periods.The SoundHound voice AI agents manage calls for pizza, take orders, answer menu questions, provide information on specials and promotions, and assist with common store inquiries. The technology is trained on Casey's menu and understands natural speech, enabling a conversational ordering experience for guests.Satturu noted that the expanded partnership allows Casey's to scale a proven solution across thousands of locations to improve guest satisfaction and operational efficiency, while Bellettini highlighted the ongoing collaboration as a demonstration of the technology's reliability.

+ CASEY’S GENERAL STORES, INC. (NASDAQ:CASY) EXTENDS SOUNDHOUND AI PARTNERSHIP TO STREAMLINE ORDERING AT CASEY’S

Casey’s General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:CASY) has renewed and expanded its SoundHound AI partnership, extending the use of AI-powered ordering across more than 2,600 locations.The ordering agents have managed more than 21 million guest interactions, improving efficiency during busy periods.Sanjeev Satturu, Casey’s Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer, says the partnership frees up staff to focus on food preparation and guest service while delivering a smoother ordering experience. SoundHound’s menu-tailored voice AI handles orders and answers questions about items, specials, and stores, with Ben Bellettini noting the scale demonstrates the platform’s reliability

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JHH-T8D3-S949-V3CC-00000-00PILOT COMPANY - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE2026-05-06T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILOT COMPANY - INDUSTRY AND PEER INSIGHTS: UP-TO-DATE

DASHBOARD: PILOT COMPANY

Websitehttps://www.pilotcompany.com/IndustryTravel Stops & ConvenienceAddress5508 Lonas Dr, Knoxville, Tennessee 37909, USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pilot Company, founded in 1958 by James Haslam II, is a leading North American travel center operator headquartered in Knoxville, Tennessee. Employing over 30,000 people, it operates 800+ Pilot and Flying J locations across 44 states and Canada, generating $30 billion annually. Providing fuel, food, and truck services, it serves 1.3 million customers daily. In 2024, Pilot expanded its EV charging network and partnered with GM for sustainability initiatives. Its culture emphasizes hospitality, safety, and community, with training in customer service and logistics. Competing with Loves Travel Stops, Pilot stands out for its scale and Berkshire Hathaways 80% ownership (Haslam family retains 20%). Recent developments include digital loyalty apps and $1 billion in store remodels, aligning with travel and eco-trends. Pilots charitable arm supports veterans and education, reinforcing its community focus.

+ PILOT COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ FLYING J REMODEL IN SOUTH BELOIT SPARKS $10,000 EDUCATION DONATIONS

On Mar. 9, 2026, Pilot Company unveiled a remodeled Flying J travel center in South Beloit and pledged $10,000 to local schools under its Miles Good program.The donations will support Hononegah's Stateline robotics program and upgrades to Prairie Hill's playground facilities.The renovation at 16049 Willowbrook Road features an expanded kitchen, a new deli area, upgraded laundry facilities, and self-checkout options, reinforcing the company's commitment to quality service and community investment.

+ PILOT EATS INTRODUCES DUAL-BRAND STRATEGY TO ELEVATE TRAVEL CENTER MEALS

Pilot Company has rolled out Pilot Eats, a new proprietary brand to expand freshly prepared meals at its North American travel centers, with the rollout starting 6th March 2026. The two concepts—Pilot Eats at about 400 centers and Pilot Eats Express at about 200—will be supported by a 24-hour operation at nearly 350 owned-and-operated quick-service locations. Pilot Eats will feature items such as a Southern chicken sandwich with a bigger crispy fillet and Mega Cheese pepperoni pizza by the slice or whole pie, while Express will emphasize grab-and-go options. The company plans a phased 2026 rollout, starting with redesigned stores in Chicopee, Mass., and Ponce de Leon, Fla., and will update packaging and signage in the spring. The Pilot Flying J App will integrate both menus, enabling mobile ordering and loyalty rewards. Promotional deals include a $6 breakfast combo and an $8 pizza-focused lunch or dinner.

+ PILOT COMPANY ACCELERATES EV INFRASTRUCTURE AS NETWORK EXPANDS

Feb. 3 Southern Tire Mart at Pilot now offers maintenance services at more than 130 locations nationwide, including 44 new sites. The EV charging network has surpassed 1,000 charging stalls at about 250 locations, with 27 new sites added since October, as Pilot advances its low carbon fueling and renewable energy initiatives. Founded in 1958 and based in Knoxville, Pilot Company operates the largest travel center network in North America with over 900 locations in 44 states and five Canadian provinces, serving roughly 1.2 million guests daily as a Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QUIKTRIP [3 News That Matters]

+ QUIKTRIP ROLLS OUT GEN 4 DESIGN WITH LIGHT, ACCESSIBILITY AND KITCHEN INNOVATION

Apr. 21, 2026 — QuikTrip unveiled its Gen 4 store concept in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, marking its most significant store update since 2010. The 6,300-square-foot Gen 4 sits between the brand's traditional layout and the travel-center model, offering a balanced, modern shopping space. Key features include enhanced storefront glass, a glass vestibule for natural light and better sightlines, and a streamlined checkout layout with eight family-friendly stalls, ADA-compliant facilities and baby-changing stations. Developed over nearly three years with more than 750 discussions, Gen 4 will roll out to future stores in Joplin, MO; Wichita, KS; and Tulsa, OK, with each new location expected to create about two dozen jobs.

+ BROKEN ARROW WELCOMES QUIKTRIP GEN 4 STORE AIMING TO IMPROVE CONVENIENCE AND FOOD ACCESS

QuikTrip has opened its first Gen 4 store in Broken Arrow, a $1.2 million investment at 5230 E. Kenosha (71st) St on 21st April 2026. The Gen 4 concept emphasizes convenience with eight bathrooms and a streamlined shopping experience. An open house featured samples from corporate chef Ryan Boone and QT employee Xochitl Reza, with attendance from media and local officials including Mayor Debra Wimpee. The project is part of efforts to improve food access in northeast Tulsa, identified as a food desert, supported by the Tulsa Metropolitan Ministry, and timed to the Route 66 100th anniversary, with broader local development planned by the Tulsa Public Facilities Authority.

+ COFFEE WOW SPURS QUIKTRIP’S SPOTLIGHT ON FAST COMPANY’S INNOVATION LIST

QuikTrip has earned 41st place on Fast Company's 2026 Most Innovative Companies list in the restaurants, dining and food services category, with the announcement dated Apr. 7, 2026.The spotlight is on the Coffee Wow tap system, offering 12 options, including six hot and six cold varieties, designed to boost the customer experience.The system delivers freshly brewed bean-to-cup coffee with real milk and sugar flavors, according to Aisha Jefferson, QuikTrip's corporate communications manager. QuikTrip, founded in 1958, is privately held with revenues above $11 billion and more than 31,000 employees; the company's sustained focus on innovation aligns with its commitment to delivering high-quality products and services.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SHEETZ [3 News That Matters]

+ SHEETZ UNVEILS FRASER STORE UNDER $8 MILLION INVESTMENT, ADDS PARK AND PLAZA

Sheetz, the Altoona-based convenience store and fuel chain, is expanding in Michigan with a Fraser location opening on May 8 at 32971 Utica Road, adding to its plan to open 14 stores in the state this year.The grand opening events will include a ribbon-cutting ceremony and free giveaways of gift cards and self-serve beverages throughout the day.In addition, a companion Fraser site will also feature a historic facade preservation alongside the Taylor location at 20623 Eureka Road, part of an $8 million investment designed to blend modern convenience with local heritage.The Fraser store will also include a 3,000-square-foot public park overlooking a detention pond and a pedestrian plaza with a monument commemorating the former bank, highlighting Sheetz's commitment to community amenities as it grows in Macomb County.

+ SHEETZ ADVANCES SUSTAINABLE RETAIL WITH 20% ENERGY SAVINGS AND EV INFRASTRUCTURE

Sheetz has achieved a 20% reduction in energy use across its 830-plus locations, announced ahead of Earth Day on 21st April 2026.Since 2019, Sheetz has been part of the U.S. Department of Energy’s Better Buildings Challenge, which aims to improve energy efficiency in commercial buildings. "For nearly 75 years, our company has been committed to making a positive impact in the communities we serve," said Eric McCrum.The Better Buildings Challenge spans about 300 organizations across nine sectors, including 24 Fortune 100 companies. Since its inception in 2011, the initiative has generated $24.2 billion in cost savings, conserved 25 billion gallons of water, and diverted 4.4 million tons of waste from landfills, according to Sheetz.To reach its goal, Sheetz installed LED lighting and low-flow fixtures in new stores and retrofitted older locations with high-efficiency HVAC systems, while more than 95% of stores actively recycle cardboard, plastics, metals, glass, and paper. The company has also expanded EV charging at more than 130 sites and offers E85 and E15 at over 450 locations, with plans to be recognized at the summit in May.

+ SHEETZ LAUNCHES STAPLES MILL ROAD STORE IN HENRICO, HIGHLIGHTING COMMUNITY PROGRAMS

Sheetz opened its Henrico store on April 16 at 7520 Staples Mill Road, a move that makes it the fifth Sheetz in Henrico County and underscores the company's ongoing regional growth.On 28th April 2026, the company contributed $2,500 to Feed More and $2,500 to Special Olympics of Virginia, with Emily Sheetz, a representative of the founding family, on hand to support the initiatives.Local residents participated in activities, and Phong Nguyen won the drawing for \"Free Sheetz for a Year\", receiving a $2,500 gift card, while two other participants were awarded $250 gift cards.Located across from the Amtrak station, the Staples Mill Road location is designed to serve both residents and travelers, combining convenience with a menu that reflects diverse preferences and a broader strategy to expand service areas while strengthening community ties.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WAWA [3 News That Matters]

+ FIRST WAWA SET TO OPEN IN MIFFLIN COUNTY, PROMISING CONVENIENCE AND COMMUNITY IMPACT

Mifflin County is preparing to welcome its first Wawa this week, with final preparations underway ahead of the grand opening. As of 28th April 2026, the iconic convenience chain is set to establish a new hub for residents and visitors.Located in a strategic spot, the store is expected to enhance daily convenience and broaden Wawa's footprint in central Pennsylvania. Customers can anticipate a menu featuring freshly prepared sandwiches, coffee, and a range of snacks, underscoring the brand's emphasis on quality and service.Local officials see potential economic benefits, including job creation and increased foot traffic that could benefit the surrounding community.The grand opening is expected to invite promotions and activities that celebrate the occasion, while signaling Wawa's ongoing growth in the region and its focus on community engagement and customer satisfaction.

+ WAWA TESTS ULTRA-FAST FLY THRU PICKUPS WHILE EXPANDING TRAVEL CENTERS

Wawa is expanding its convenience-focused strategy with 30-second Fly Thru pickup windows at select sites as part of a plan to add about 95 new stores in 2026. The Fly Thru concept lets customers order via the Wawa app and pick up without leaving their vehicles. Current deployment includes three Midwest stores and two locations in Langhorne, Pa., and New Jersey. CEO Brian Schaller has underscored the potential of the service, while noting that extending Fly Thru to existing stores will require time and coordination with local authorities. In parallel, Wawa is growing a travel-center footprint for truckers with openings in Indiana and Ohio, described by CEO Chris Gheysens as "Wawa on steroids" with larger stores and improved parking. With roughly 1,200 locations and ongoing renovations, the company says these moves align with a broader shift toward speed and mobile ordering. A related note: a viral discussion about Wawa’s hoagies arose after a book reference by author B.K. Borison.

+ FRANKLIN COUNTY TO WELCOME WAWA WITH A NEW CHAMBERSBURG LOCATION

Wawa is planning a new convenience store in Chambersburg, Franklin County, targeting the Westside Plaza site at 875 Lincoln Way West. The announcement, dated Apr. 21, 2026, said the company is evaluating the property and pursuing the necessary permits.The project would place a stand-alone building on the former Wendy's site, alongside the shopping center's parking area.Once approved, the store would join Surplus City and Brother's Pizza at Westside Plaza, with two vacant spaces ranging from 1,600 to 2,400 square feet indicating room for further growth in the area. Wawa operates over 1,110 stores across several states, employs about 47,000 associates and serves roughly 1 billion customers annually.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CASEY'S GENERAL STORES [3 News That Matters]

+ CASEY'S GENERAL STORES, INC. (NASDAQ:CASY) BROADENS USE OF SOUNDHOUND VOICE AI ACROSS ITS NETWORK

Casey's General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:CASY) expands its partnership with SoundHound AI Inc. to deploy voice ordering agents across more than 2,600 locations, effective 29th April 2026. The move follows SoundHound's role in handling over 21 million guest interactions to date and aims to enhance guest service and store efficiency during peak ordering periods.The SoundHound voice AI agents manage calls for pizza, take orders, answer menu questions, provide information on specials and promotions, and assist with common store inquiries. The technology is trained on Casey's menu and understands natural speech, enabling a conversational ordering experience for guests.Satturu noted that the expanded partnership allows Casey's to scale a proven solution across thousands of locations to improve guest satisfaction and operational efficiency, while Bellettini highlighted the ongoing collaboration as a demonstration of the technology's reliability.

+ CASEY’S GENERAL STORES, INC. (NASDAQ:CASY) EXTENDS SOUNDHOUND AI PARTNERSHIP TO STREAMLINE ORDERING AT CASEY’S

Casey’s General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:CASY) has renewed and expanded its SoundHound AI partnership, extending the use of AI-powered ordering across more than 2,600 locations.The ordering agents have managed more than 21 million guest interactions, improving efficiency during busy periods.Sanjeev Satturu, Casey’s Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer, says the partnership frees up staff to focus on food preparation and guest service while delivering a smoother ordering experience. SoundHound’s menu-tailored voice AI handles orders and answers questions about items, specials, and stores, with Ben Bellettini noting the scale demonstrates the platform’s reliability

+ CASEY’S GENERAL STORES INTEGRATES FIKES WHOLESALE, EXTENDS SOUTHERN PRESENCE AMID THREE-YEAR PLAN

Casey’s General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ: CASY) has evolved from a Midwest staple into a national rural retailer with more than 2,900 locations across 20 states. It recently completed its largest acquisition to date, Fikes Wholesale, and concluded its 2023-2026 plan to boost efficiency and geographic reach.Founded in 1968 by Donald Lamberti in Boone, Iowa, Casey’s began as a small garage and went public in 1983. The mid-1980s decision to introduce made-from-scratch pizza helped Casey’s become the fifth-largest pizza chain in the United States, while maintaining its community focus.Casey’s derives revenue from three pillars — Fuel, Grocery & General Merchandise, and Prepared Food & Dispensed Beverages — and owns roughly 60% of its real estate. Fuel drives traffic but carries lower margins, while the prepared foods business generates margins above 60% and fuels profitability. About half of stores operate in towns with 5,000 or fewer residents, creating a defensible moat against larger urban rivals.The company has delivered steady shareholder value, with a roughly 14% compound annual growth rate over the past decade and a 22% gain in the last year, supported by the CEFCO acquisition and Texas expansion. As Casey’s approaches a new three-year plan, it remains focused on high-margin offerings and digital loyalty while managing fuel demand trends and labor costs.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
