urn:contentItem:6JHB-0NR3-RVT7-63C7-00000-00PILOT COMPANY - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILOT COMPANY - INDUSTRY AND PEER TRENDS: THE RECENT

DASHBOARD: PILOT COMPANY

Websitehttps://www.pilotcompany.com/IndustryTravel Stops & ConvenienceAddress5508 Lonas Dr, Knoxville, Tennessee 37909, USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pilot Company, founded in 1958 by James Haslam II, is a leading North American travel center operator headquartered in Knoxville, Tennessee. Employing over 30,000 people, it operates 800+ Pilot and Flying J locations across 44 states and Canada, generating $30 billion annually. Providing fuel, food, and truck services, it serves 1.3 million customers daily. In 2024, Pilot expanded its EV charging network and partnered with GM for sustainability initiatives. Its culture emphasizes hospitality, safety, and community, with training in customer service and logistics. Competing with Loves Travel Stops, Pilot stands out for its scale and Berkshire Hathaways 80% ownership (Haslam family retains 20%). Recent developments include digital loyalty apps and $1 billion in store remodels, aligning with travel and eco-trends. Pilots charitable arm supports veterans and education, reinforcing its community focus.

+ PILOT COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ FLYING J REMODEL IN SOUTH BELOIT SPARKS $10,000 EDUCATION DONATIONS

On Mar. 9, 2026, Pilot Company unveiled a remodeled Flying J travel center in South Beloit and pledged $10,000 to local schools under its Miles Good program.The donations will support Hononegah's Stateline robotics program and upgrades to Prairie Hill's playground facilities.The renovation at 16049 Willowbrook Road features an expanded kitchen, a new deli area, upgraded laundry facilities, and self-checkout options, reinforcing the company's commitment to quality service and community investment.

+ PILOT EATS INTRODUCES DUAL-BRAND STRATEGY TO ELEVATE TRAVEL CENTER MEALS

Pilot Company has rolled out Pilot Eats, a new proprietary brand to expand freshly prepared meals at its North American travel centers, with the rollout starting 6th March 2026. The two concepts—Pilot Eats at about 400 centers and Pilot Eats Express at about 200—will be supported by a 24-hour operation at nearly 350 owned-and-operated quick-service locations. Pilot Eats will feature items such as a Southern chicken sandwich with a bigger crispy fillet and Mega Cheese pepperoni pizza by the slice or whole pie, while Express will emphasize grab-and-go options. The company plans a phased 2026 rollout, starting with redesigned stores in Chicopee, Mass., and Ponce de Leon, Fla., and will update packaging and signage in the spring. The Pilot Flying J App will integrate both menus, enabling mobile ordering and loyalty rewards. Promotional deals include a $6 breakfast combo and an $8 pizza-focused lunch or dinner.

+ PILOT COMPANY ACCELERATES EV INFRASTRUCTURE AS NETWORK EXPANDS

Feb. 3 Southern Tire Mart at Pilot now offers maintenance services at more than 130 locations nationwide, including 44 new sites. The EV charging network has surpassed 1,000 charging stalls at about 250 locations, with 27 new sites added since October, as Pilot advances its low carbon fueling and renewable energy initiatives. Founded in 1958 and based in Knoxville, Pilot Company operates the largest travel center network in North America with over 900 locations in 44 states and five Canadian provinces, serving roughly 1.2 million guests daily as a Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QUIKTRIP [3 News That Matters]

+ QUIKTRIP ROLLS OUT GEN 4 DESIGN WITH LIGHT, ACCESSIBILITY AND KITCHEN INNOVATION

Apr. 21, 2026 — QuikTrip unveiled its Gen 4 store concept in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, marking its most significant store update since 2010. The 6,300-square-foot Gen 4 sits between the brand's traditional layout and the travel-center model, offering a balanced, modern shopping space. Key features include enhanced storefront glass, a glass vestibule for natural light and better sightlines, and a streamlined checkout layout with eight family-friendly stalls, ADA-compliant facilities and baby-changing stations. Developed over nearly three years with more than 750 discussions, Gen 4 will roll out to future stores in Joplin, MO; Wichita, KS; and Tulsa, OK, with each new location expected to create about two dozen jobs.

+ BROKEN ARROW WELCOMES QUIKTRIP GEN 4 STORE AIMING TO IMPROVE CONVENIENCE AND FOOD ACCESS

QuikTrip has opened its first Gen 4 store in Broken Arrow, a $1.2 million investment at 5230 E. Kenosha (71st) St on 21st April 2026. The Gen 4 concept emphasizes convenience with eight bathrooms and a streamlined shopping experience. An open house featured samples from corporate chef Ryan Boone and QT employee Xochitl Reza, with attendance from media and local officials including Mayor Debra Wimpee. The project is part of efforts to improve food access in northeast Tulsa, identified as a food desert, supported by the Tulsa Metropolitan Ministry, and timed to the Route 66 100th anniversary, with broader local development planned by the Tulsa Public Facilities Authority.

+ COFFEE WOW SPURS QUIKTRIP’S SPOTLIGHT ON FAST COMPANY’S INNOVATION LIST

QuikTrip has earned 41st place on Fast Company's 2026 Most Innovative Companies list in the restaurants, dining and food services category, with the announcement dated Apr. 7, 2026.The spotlight is on the Coffee Wow tap system, offering 12 options, including six hot and six cold varieties, designed to boost the customer experience.The system delivers freshly brewed bean-to-cup coffee with real milk and sugar flavors, according to Aisha Jefferson, QuikTrip's corporate communications manager. QuikTrip, founded in 1958, is privately held with revenues above $11 billion and more than 31,000 employees; the company's sustained focus on innovation aligns with its commitment to delivering high-quality products and services.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SHEETZ [3 News That Matters]

+ SHEETZ UNVEILS FRASER STORE UNDER $8 MILLION INVESTMENT, ADDS PARK AND PLAZA

Sheetz, the Altoona-based convenience store and fuel chain, is expanding in Michigan with a Fraser location opening on May 8 at 32971 Utica Road, adding to its plan to open 14 stores in the state this year.The grand opening events will include a ribbon-cutting ceremony and free giveaways of gift cards and self-serve beverages throughout the day.In addition, a companion Fraser site will also feature a historic facade preservation alongside the Taylor location at 20623 Eureka Road, part of an $8 million investment designed to blend modern convenience with local heritage.The Fraser store will also include a 3,000-square-foot public park overlooking a detention pond and a pedestrian plaza with a monument commemorating the former bank, highlighting Sheetz's commitment to community amenities as it grows in Macomb County.

+ SHEETZ ADVANCES SUSTAINABLE RETAIL WITH 20% ENERGY SAVINGS AND EV INFRASTRUCTURE

Sheetz has achieved a 20% reduction in energy use across its 830-plus locations, announced ahead of Earth Day on 21st April 2026.Since 2019, Sheetz has been part of the U.S. Department of Energy’s Better Buildings Challenge, which aims to improve energy efficiency in commercial buildings. "For nearly 75 years, our company has been committed to making a positive impact in the communities we serve," said Eric McCrum.The Better Buildings Challenge spans about 300 organizations across nine sectors, including 24 Fortune 100 companies. Since its inception in 2011, the initiative has generated $24.2 billion in cost savings, conserved 25 billion gallons of water, and diverted 4.4 million tons of waste from landfills, according to Sheetz.To reach its goal, Sheetz installed LED lighting and low-flow fixtures in new stores and retrofitted older locations with high-efficiency HVAC systems, while more than 95% of stores actively recycle cardboard, plastics, metals, glass, and paper. The company has also expanded EV charging at more than 130 sites and offers E85 and E15 at over 450 locations, with plans to be recognized at the summit in May.

+ SHEETZ LAUNCHES STAPLES MILL ROAD STORE IN HENRICO, HIGHLIGHTING COMMUNITY PROGRAMS

Sheetz opened its Henrico store on April 16 at 7520 Staples Mill Road, a move that makes it the fifth Sheetz in Henrico County and underscores the company's ongoing regional growth.On 28th April 2026, the company contributed $2,500 to Feed More and $2,500 to Special Olympics of Virginia, with Emily Sheetz, a representative of the founding family, on hand to support the initiatives.Local residents participated in activities, and Phong Nguyen won the drawing for \"Free Sheetz for a Year\", receiving a $2,500 gift card, while two other participants were awarded $250 gift cards.Located across from the Amtrak station, the Staples Mill Road location is designed to serve both residents and travelers, combining convenience with a menu that reflects diverse preferences and a broader strategy to expand service areas while strengthening community ties.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WAWA [3 News That Matters]

+ FIRST WAWA SET TO OPEN IN MIFFLIN COUNTY, PROMISING CONVENIENCE AND COMMUNITY IMPACT

Mifflin County is preparing to welcome its first Wawa this week, with final preparations underway ahead of the grand opening. As of 28th April 2026, the iconic convenience chain is set to establish a new hub for residents and visitors.Located in a strategic spot, the store is expected to enhance daily convenience and broaden Wawa's footprint in central Pennsylvania. Customers can anticipate a menu featuring freshly prepared sandwiches, coffee, and a range of snacks, underscoring the brand's emphasis on quality and service.Local officials see potential economic benefits, including job creation and increased foot traffic that could benefit the surrounding community.The grand opening is expected to invite promotions and activities that celebrate the occasion, while signaling Wawa's ongoing growth in the region and its focus on community engagement and customer satisfaction.

+ WAWA TESTS ULTRA-FAST FLY THRU PICKUPS WHILE EXPANDING TRAVEL CENTERS

Wawa is expanding its convenience-focused strategy with 30-second Fly Thru pickup windows at select sites as part of a plan to add about 95 new stores in 2026. The Fly Thru concept lets customers order via the Wawa app and pick up without leaving their vehicles. Current deployment includes three Midwest stores and two locations in Langhorne, Pa., and New Jersey. CEO Brian Schaller has underscored the potential of the service, while noting that extending Fly Thru to existing stores will require time and coordination with local authorities. In parallel, Wawa is growing a travel-center footprint for truckers with openings in Indiana and Ohio, described by CEO Chris Gheysens as "Wawa on steroids" with larger stores and improved parking. With roughly 1,200 locations and ongoing renovations, the company says these moves align with a broader shift toward speed and mobile ordering. A related note: a viral discussion about Wawa’s hoagies arose after a book reference by author B.K. Borison.

+ FRANKLIN COUNTY TO WELCOME WAWA WITH A NEW CHAMBERSBURG LOCATION

Wawa is planning a new convenience store in Chambersburg, Franklin County, targeting the Westside Plaza site at 875 Lincoln Way West. The announcement, dated Apr. 21, 2026, said the company is evaluating the property and pursuing the necessary permits.The project would place a stand-alone building on the former Wendy's site, alongside the shopping center's parking area.Once approved, the store would join Surplus City and Brother's Pizza at Westside Plaza, with two vacant spaces ranging from 1,600 to 2,400 square feet indicating room for further growth in the area. Wawa operates over 1,110 stores across several states, employs about 47,000 associates and serves roughly 1 billion customers annually.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CASEY'S GENERAL STORES [3 News That Matters]

+ CASEY'S GENERAL STORES, INC. (NASDAQ:CASY) BROADENS USE OF SOUNDHOUND VOICE AI ACROSS ITS NETWORK

Casey's General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:CASY) expands its partnership with SoundHound AI Inc. to deploy voice ordering agents across more than 2,600 locations, effective 29th April 2026. The move follows SoundHound's role in handling over 21 million guest interactions to date and aims to enhance guest service and store efficiency during peak ordering periods.The SoundHound voice AI agents manage calls for pizza, take orders, answer menu questions, provide information on specials and promotions, and assist with common store inquiries. The technology is trained on Casey's menu and understands natural speech, enabling a conversational ordering experience for guests.Satturu noted that the expanded partnership allows Casey's to scale a proven solution across thousands of locations to improve guest satisfaction and operational efficiency, while Bellettini highlighted the ongoing collaboration as a demonstration of the technology's reliability.

+ CASEY’S GENERAL STORES, INC. (NASDAQ:CASY) EXTENDS SOUNDHOUND AI PARTNERSHIP TO STREAMLINE ORDERING AT CASEY’S

Casey’s General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:CASY) has renewed and expanded its SoundHound AI partnership, extending the use of AI-powered ordering across more than 2,600 locations.The ordering agents have managed more than 21 million guest interactions, improving efficiency during busy periods.Sanjeev Satturu, Casey’s Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer, says the partnership frees up staff to focus on food preparation and guest service while delivering a smoother ordering experience. SoundHound’s menu-tailored voice AI handles orders and answers questions about items, specials, and stores, with Ben Bellettini noting the scale demonstrates the platform’s reliability

+ CASEY’S GENERAL STORES INTEGRATES FIKES WHOLESALE, EXTENDS SOUTHERN PRESENCE AMID THREE-YEAR PLAN

Casey’s General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ: CASY) has evolved from a Midwest staple into a national rural retailer with more than 2,900 locations across 20 states. It recently completed its largest acquisition to date, Fikes Wholesale, and concluded its 2023-2026 plan to boost efficiency and geographic reach.Founded in 1968 by Donald Lamberti in Boone, Iowa, Casey’s began as a small garage and went public in 1983. The mid-1980s decision to introduce made-from-scratch pizza helped Casey’s become the fifth-largest pizza chain in the United States, while maintaining its community focus.Casey’s derives revenue from three pillars — Fuel, Grocery & General Merchandise, and Prepared Food & Dispensed Beverages — and owns roughly 60% of its real estate. Fuel drives traffic but carries lower margins, while the prepared foods business generates margins above 60% and fuels profitability. About half of stores operate in towns with 5,000 or fewer residents, creating a defensible moat against larger urban rivals.The company has delivered steady shareholder value, with a roughly 14% compound annual growth rate over the past decade and a 22% gain in the last year, supported by the CEFCO acquisition and Texas expansion. As Casey’s approaches a new three-year plan, it remains focused on high-margin offerings and digital loyalty while managing fuel demand trends and labor costs.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

"For nearly 75 years, our company has been committed to making a positive impact in the communities we serve," said Eric McCrum.The Better Buildings Challenge spans about 300 organizations across nine sectors, including 24 Fortune 100 companies. Since its inception in 2011, the initiative has generated $24.2 billion in cost savings, conserved 25 billion gallons of water, and diverted 4.4 million tons of waste from landfills, according to Sheetz.To reach its goal, Sheetz installed LED lighting and low-flow fixtures in new stores and retrofitted older locations with high-efficiency HVAC systems, while more than 95% of stores actively recycle cardboard, plastics, metals, glass, and paper. The company has also expanded EV charging at more than 130 sites and offers E85 and E15 at over 450 locations, with plans to be recognized at the summit in May.

+ SHEETZ LAUNCHES STAPLES MILL ROAD STORE IN HENRICO, HIGHLIGHTING COMMUNITY PROGRAMS

Sheetz opened its Henrico store on April 16 at 7520 Staples Mill Road, a move that makes it the fifth Sheetz in Henrico County and underscores the company's ongoing regional growth.On 28th April 2026, the company contributed $2,500 to Feed More and $2,500 to Special Olympics of Virginia, with Emily Sheetz, a representative of the founding family, on hand to support the initiatives.Local residents participated in activities, and Phong Nguyen won the drawing for \"Free Sheetz for a Year\", receiving a $2,500 gift card, while two other participants were awarded $250 gift cards.Located across from the Amtrak station, the Staples Mill Road location is designed to serve both residents and travelers, combining convenience with a menu that reflects diverse preferences and a broader strategy to expand service areas while strengthening community ties.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ WAWA [3 News That Matters]

+ FIRST WAWA SET TO OPEN IN MIFFLIN COUNTY, PROMISING CONVENIENCE AND COMMUNITY IMPACT

Mifflin County is preparing to welcome its first Wawa this week, with final preparations underway ahead of the grand opening. As of 28th April 2026, the iconic convenience chain is set to establish a new hub for residents and visitors.Located in a strategic spot, the store is expected to enhance daily convenience and broaden Wawa's footprint in central Pennsylvania. Customers can anticipate a menu featuring freshly prepared sandwiches, coffee, and a range of snacks, underscoring the brand's emphasis on quality and service.Local officials see potential economic benefits, including job creation and increased foot traffic that could benefit the surrounding community.The grand opening is expected to invite promotions and activities that celebrate the occasion, while signaling Wawa's ongoing growth in the region and its focus on community engagement and customer satisfaction.

+ WAWA TESTS ULTRA-FAST FLY THRU PICKUPS WHILE EXPANDING TRAVEL CENTERS

Wawa is expanding its convenience-focused strategy with 30-second Fly Thru pickup windows at select sites as part of a plan to add about 95 new stores in 2026. The Fly Thru concept lets customers order via the Wawa app and pick up without leaving their vehicles. Current deployment includes three Midwest stores and two locations in Langhorne, Pa., and New Jersey. CEO Brian Schaller has underscored the potential of the service, while noting that extending Fly Thru to existing stores will require time and coordination with local authorities. In parallel, Wawa is growing a travel-center footprint for truckers with openings in Indiana and Ohio, described by CEO Chris Gheysens as "Wawa on steroids" with larger stores and improved parking. With roughly 1,200 locations and ongoing renovations, the company says these moves align with a broader shift toward speed and mobile ordering. A related note: a viral discussion about Wawa’s hoagies arose after a book reference by author B.K. Borison.

+ FRANKLIN COUNTY TO WELCOME WAWA WITH A NEW CHAMBERSBURG LOCATION

Wawa is planning a new convenience store in Chambersburg, Franklin County, targeting the Westside Plaza site at 875 Lincoln Way West. The announcement, dated Apr. 21, 2026, said the company is evaluating the property and pursuing the necessary permits.The project would place a stand-alone building on the former Wendy's site, alongside the shopping center's parking area.Once approved, the store would join Surplus City and Brother's Pizza at Westside Plaza, with two vacant spaces ranging from 1,600 to 2,400 square feet indicating room for further growth in the area. Wawa operates over 1,110 stores across several states, employs about 47,000 associates and serves roughly 1 billion customers annually.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CASEY'S GENERAL STORES [3 News That Matters]

+ CASEY'S GENERAL STORES, INC. (NASDAQ:CASY) BROADENS USE OF SOUNDHOUND VOICE AI ACROSS ITS NETWORK

Casey's General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:CASY) expands its partnership with SoundHound AI Inc. to deploy voice ordering agents across more than 2,600 locations, effective 29th April 2026. The move follows SoundHound's role in handling over 21 million guest interactions to date and aims to enhance guest service and store efficiency during peak ordering periods.The SoundHound voice AI agents manage calls for pizza, take orders, answer menu questions, provide information on specials and promotions, and assist with common store inquiries. The technology is trained on Casey's menu and understands natural speech, enabling a conversational ordering experience for guests.Satturu noted that the expanded partnership allows Casey's to scale a proven solution across thousands of locations to improve guest satisfaction and operational efficiency, while Bellettini highlighted the ongoing collaboration as a demonstration of the technology's reliability.

+ CASEY’S GENERAL STORES, INC. (NASDAQ:CASY) EXTENDS SOUNDHOUND AI PARTNERSHIP TO STREAMLINE ORDERING AT CASEY’S

Casey’s General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ:CASY) has renewed and expanded its SoundHound AI partnership, extending the use of AI-powered ordering across more than 2,600 locations.The ordering agents have managed more than 21 million guest interactions, improving efficiency during busy periods.Sanjeev Satturu, Casey’s Senior Vice President and Chief Information Officer, says the partnership frees up staff to focus on food preparation and guest service while delivering a smoother ordering experience. SoundHound’s menu-tailored voice AI handles orders and answers questions about items, specials, and stores, with Ben Bellettini noting the scale demonstrates the platform’s reliability

+ CASEY’S GENERAL STORES INTEGRATES FIKES WHOLESALE, EXTENDS SOUTHERN PRESENCE AMID THREE-YEAR PLAN

Casey’s General Stores, Inc. (NASDAQ: CASY) has evolved from a Midwest staple into a national rural retailer with more than 2,900 locations across 20 states. It recently completed its largest acquisition to date, Fikes Wholesale, and concluded its 2023-2026 plan to boost efficiency and geographic reach.Founded in 1968 by Donald Lamberti in Boone, Iowa, Casey’s began as a small garage and went public in 1983. The mid-1980s decision to introduce made-from-scratch pizza helped Casey’s become the fifth-largest pizza chain in the United States, while maintaining its community focus.Casey’s derives revenue from three pillars — Fuel, Grocery & General Merchandise, and Prepared Food & Dispensed Beverages — and owns roughly 60% of its real estate. Fuel drives traffic but carries lower margins, while the prepared foods business generates margins above 60% and fuels profitability. About half of stores operate in towns with 5,000 or fewer residents, creating a defensible moat against larger urban rivals.The company has delivered steady shareholder value, with a roughly 14% compound annual growth rate over the past decade and a 22% gain in the last year, supported by the CEFCO acquisition and Texas expansion. As Casey’s approaches a new three-year plan, it remains focused on high-margin offerings and digital loyalty while managing fuel demand trends and labor costs.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

urn:contentItem:6JGG-3XS3-S7C2-R1HF-00000-00PILOT COMPANY - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisSTOCKPILOT COMPANY - RECENT PEER AND INDUSTRY TRENDS

DASHBOARD: PILOT COMPANY

Websitehttps://www.pilotcompany.com/IndustryTravel Stops & ConvenienceAddress5508 Lonas Dr, Knoxville, Tennessee 37909, USA

ACTIVITIES:

Pilot Company, founded in 1958 by James Haslam II, is a leading North American travel center operator headquartered in Knoxville, Tennessee. Employing over 30,000 people, it operates 800+ Pilot and Flying J locations across 44 states and Canada, generating $30 billion annually. Providing fuel, food, and truck services, it serves 1.3 million customers daily. In 2024, Pilot expanded its EV charging network and partnered with GM for sustainability initiatives. Its culture emphasizes hospitality, safety, and community, with training in customer service and logistics. Competing with Loves Travel Stops, Pilot stands out for its scale and Berkshire Hathaways 80% ownership (Haslam family retains 20%). Recent developments include digital loyalty apps and $1 billion in store remodels, aligning with travel and eco-trends. Pilots charitable arm supports veterans and education, reinforcing its community focus.

+ PILOT COMPANY [3 News That Matters]

+ FLYING J REMODEL IN SOUTH BELOIT SPARKS $10,000 EDUCATION DONATIONS

On Mar. 9, 2026, Pilot Company unveiled a remodeled Flying J travel center in South Beloit and pledged $10,000 to local schools under its Miles Good program.The donations will support Hononegah's Stateline robotics program and upgrades to Prairie Hill's playground facilities.The renovation at 16049 Willowbrook Road features an expanded kitchen, a new deli area, upgraded laundry facilities, and self-checkout options, reinforcing the company's commitment to quality service and community investment.

+ PILOT EATS INTRODUCES DUAL-BRAND STRATEGY TO ELEVATE TRAVEL CENTER MEALS

Pilot Company has rolled out Pilot Eats, a new proprietary brand to expand freshly prepared meals at its North American travel centers, with the rollout starting 6th March 2026. The two concepts—Pilot Eats at about 400 centers and Pilot Eats Express at about 200—will be supported by a 24-hour operation at nearly 350 owned-and-operated quick-service locations. Pilot Eats will feature items such as a Southern chicken sandwich with a bigger crispy fillet and Mega Cheese pepperoni pizza by the slice or whole pie, while Express will emphasize grab-and-go options. The company plans a phased 2026 rollout, starting with redesigned stores in Chicopee, Mass., and Ponce de Leon, Fla., and will update packaging and signage in the spring. The Pilot Flying J App will integrate both menus, enabling mobile ordering and loyalty rewards. Promotional deals include a $6 breakfast combo and an $8 pizza-focused lunch or dinner.

+ PILOT COMPANY ACCELERATES EV INFRASTRUCTURE AS NETWORK EXPANDS

Feb. 3 Southern Tire Mart at Pilot now offers maintenance services at more than 130 locations nationwide, including 44 new sites. The EV charging network has surpassed 1,000 charging stalls at about 250 locations, with 27 new sites added since October, as Pilot advances its low carbon fueling and renewable energy initiatives. Founded in 1958 and based in Knoxville, Pilot Company operates the largest travel center network in North America with over 900 locations in 44 states and five Canadian provinces, serving roughly 1.2 million guests daily as a Berkshire Hathaway subsidiary.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ QUIKTRIP [3 News That Matters]

+ QUIKTRIP ROLLS OUT GEN 4 DESIGN WITH LIGHT, ACCESSIBILITY AND KITCHEN INNOVATION

Apr. 21, 2026 — QuikTrip unveiled its Gen 4 store concept in Broken Arrow, Oklahoma, marking its most significant store update since 2010. The 6,300-square-foot Gen 4 sits between the brand's traditional layout and the travel-center model, offering a balanced, modern shopping space. Key features include enhanced storefront glass, a glass vestibule for natural light and better sightlines, and a streamlined checkout layout with eight family-friendly stalls, ADA-compliant facilities and baby-changing stations. Developed over nearly three years with more than 750 discussions, Gen 4 will roll out to future stores in Joplin, MO; Wichita, KS; and Tulsa, OK, with each new location expected to create about two dozen jobs.

+ BROKEN ARROW WELCOMES QUIKTRIP GEN 4 STORE AIMING TO IMPROVE CONVENIENCE AND FOOD ACCESS

QuikTrip has opened its first Gen 4 store in Broken Arrow, a $1.2 million investment at 5230 E. Kenosha (71st) St on 21st April 2026. The Gen 4 concept emphasizes convenience with eight bathrooms and a streamlined shopping experience. An open house featured samples from corporate chef Ryan Boone and QT employee Xochitl Reza, with attendance from media and local officials including Mayor Debra Wimpee. The project is part of efforts to improve food access in northeast Tulsa, identified as a food desert, supported by the Tulsa Metropolitan Ministry, and timed to the Route 66 100th anniversary, with broader local development planned by the Tulsa Public Facilities Authority.

+ COFFEE WOW SPURS QUIKTRIP’S SPOTLIGHT ON FAST COMPANY’S INNOVATION LIST

QuikTrip has earned 41st place on Fast Company's 2026 Most Innovative Companies list in the restaurants, dining and food services category, with the announcement dated Apr. 7, 2026.The spotlight is on the Coffee Wow tap system, offering 12 options, including six hot and six cold varieties, designed to boost the customer experience.The system delivers freshly brewed bean-to-cup coffee with real milk and sugar flavors, according to Aisha Jefferson, QuikTrip's corporate communications manager. QuikTrip, founded in 1958, is privately held with revenues above $11 billion and more than 31,000 employees; the company's sustained focus on innovation aligns with its commitment to delivering high-quality products and services.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ SHEETZ [3 News That Matters]

+ SHEETZ UNVEILS FRASER STORE UNDER $8 MILLION INVESTMENT, ADDS PARK AND PLAZA

Sheetz, the Altoona-based convenience store and fuel chain, is expanding in Michigan with a Fraser location opening on May 8 at 32971 Utica Road, adding to its plan to open 14 stores in the state this year.The grand opening events will include a ribbon-cutting ceremony and free giveaways of gift cards and self-serve beverages throughout the day.In addition, a companion Fraser site will also feature a historic facade preservation alongside the Taylor location at 20623 Eureka Road, part of an $8 million investment designed to blend modern convenience with local heritage.The Fraser store will also include a 3,000-square-foot public park overlooking a detention pond and a pedestrian plaza with a monument commemorating the former bank, highlighting Sheetz's commitment to community amenities as it grows in Macomb County.

value.Title: Skyview Ventures Expands Biodiversity and Data-Driven Beekeeping Program Across Multi-State Solar Portfolio with HiveTracks

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH5-MNJ3-RXG0-614B-00000-00Skyview Ventures Expands Biodiversity and Data-Driven Beekeeping Program Across Multi-State Solar Portfolio with HiveTracks2026-05-05T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:06:57ZLexisNexisSkyview Ventures Expands Biodiversity and Data-Driven Beekeeping Program Across Multi-State Solar Portfolio with HiveTracksNASHVILLE, Tenn. and CRESTON, N.C., May 5, 2026

PR NewswireInnovative initiative integrates pollinator habitat, biodiversity monitoring, and environmental data collection across solar projectsNASHVILLE, Tenn. and CRESTON, N.C., May 5, 2026 /PRNewswire/ --Skyview Ventures, a U.S.-based renewable energy investor, together with its solar company, Davis Hill Development, today announces the expansion of its partnership withHiveTracks, a biodiversity intelligence platform, to scale data-driven environmental monitoring and managed pollinator programs across its solar portfolio. The program integrates apiaries, environmental monitoring, and pollinator habitats into operating and developing solar projects across multiple U.S. states.

Skyview Ventures is both an investor in HiveTracks and the company's first solar industry client. The collaboration began in 2025 with eight solar projects across New York, West Virginia, Tennessee, and Connecticut. Following the success of the initial rollout, Skyview Ventures plans to expand the program to additional sites in 2026."This partnership reflects our broader vision that renewable energy projects should be developed with long-term environmental stewardship in mind," said Andy Karetsky, CEO of Skyview Ventures. "By integrating biodiversity monitoring, pollinator habitats, and data-driven land management into our solar portfolio, we are demonstrating that solar projects can deliver environmental benefits beyond clean energy generation and play a meaningful role in supporting local ecosystems."Each participating solar site is managed by local beekeepers recruited and supported by HiveTracks. These apiaries serve not only for honey production but also as environmental monitoring tools, offering insight into ecosystem health and land use impact over timeHiveTracks produces environmental metrics, visualized on a per site and portfolio level, generating actionable recommendations to improve ecological conditions and biodiversity. The data collected comprise pollinator and plant diversity and abundance, and are complemented with hive-based testing for eDNA and ecotoxicology. In 2025, the honey produced at the program's apiaries was bottled and donated to food banks and given away as gifts.The program spans projects at multiple stages of development—from pre-construction through operations—allowing Skyview Ventures to evaluate environmental impact across the full lifecycle of solar project development. Following annual analysis, HiveTracks provides site-specific recommendations that allowDavis Hill Development, the project operator, to implement pollinator-friendly operations and maintenance practices, and improve habitat conditions over time."Solar energy already provides environmental benefits, but with HiveTracks, we saw an opportunity to go further," said Maria Morales Ferrebus, Solar Development Associate at Skyview Ventures. "In our first year, the data provided valuable insights into ecosystem health across our sites. For example, we identified areas with low plant diversity, which can reduce resilience to drought conditions and increase erosion risk. By implementing HiveTracks' recommendations, we can adapt our operations and maintenance practices to enhance plant diversity, improve soil health, and strengthen pollinator habitats."Together, these site-level insights are informing how Skyview Ventures approaches land stewardship across its portfolio. By integrating apiaries and environmental monitoring into its solar projects, the company is creating a scalable model that supports pollinator health, enables community agriculture, and generates consistent, site-specific data to guide long-term land management decisions."Solar sites represent a unique opportunity to support pollinators and monitor biodiversity at scale," said Max Rünzel, CEO of HiveTracks. "Our partnership with Skyview Ventures demonstrates how beekeeping, environmental data, and renewable energy infrastructure can work together to create measurable environmental impact."The expanded program reflects a broader trend in the renewable energy industry toward multi-use land strategies, where solar development is combined with agriculture, habitat restoration, and biodiversity initiatives.About Skyview VenturesSkyview Ventures has three operating businesses: Skyview Finance Company, one of the largest merchants of Environmental Attributes; Davis Hill Development, which develops, owns, and operates solar and battery storage facilities; and Skycharger, which develops, owns, and operates electric vehicle charging infrastructure. In addition, Skyview Ventures has over 30 venture investments on their portfolio.The company owns 70 MW of solar assets across 15 states and over 600 EV charging stations. Headquartered in Nashville, TN, with additional offices in Greenwich, CT and Sacramento, CA, Skyview Ventures is a national leader in clean energy.To learn more, visitwww.skyviewventures.com.About HiveTracksHiveTracks is building the data infrastructure for biodiversity, enabling landowners, developers, and farmers to measure and improve ecological outcomes directly on-site. HiveTracks brings environmental monitoring and sustainable agriculture to the green energy sector by leveraging beehives as biosensors and empowering beekeepers as field ecologists. To learn more, visitwww.hivetracks.com.About Davis Hill DevelopmentDavis Hill Development (DHD), a subsidiary of Skyview Ventures, is a leading developer and owner of distributed solar projects with more than 180 operating assets across 15 states. Founded in 2013, DHD delivers turnkey clean energy solutions for businesses, municipalities, and landowners, with a growing pipeline of community and commercial solar projects. The company specializes in structuring projects that generate long-term value—reducing energy costs, providing lease revenue, and supporting energy equity through community solar access for low-to-moderate income (LMI) residents. Through its parent company, DHD also offers REC and carbon credit solutions to help organizations meet sustainability goals. Learn more atwww.davishilldevelopment.comMedia Contact:Alexandra Pony

value.Title: Creditors in the renewable energy sector are threatening to seize the Spanish national team's assets during the World Cup

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JJ0-XM13-S4GT-B0JT-00000-00Creditors in the renewable energy sector are threatening to seize the Spanish national team's assets during the World Cup2026-05-08T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisCreditors in the renewable energy sector are threatening to seize the Spanish national team's assets during the World Cup


Creditors suing Spain over cuts to renewable energy subsidies have stepped up their pressure tactics to collect compensation awarded in international arbitration proceedings. Vulture funds that have acquired the rights to represent and collect on these claims have received the green light from U.S. courts to track down more Spanish assets and property subject to seizure throughout the United States, according to legal sources familiar with these proceedings. Thus, amid threats to block payments and collections linked to the Spanish national team's participation in the World Cup to be held in the United States, the plaintiffs have warned that they will extend seizure requests to all territories where the national team travels during the tournament.


During the second half of 2025, the U.S. District Court for the District of Columbia-theU.S. court with jurisdiction over international commercial law disputes- adopted the ruling of the Court of Appeals for that federal district, which a year earlier had rejected Spain's claim to sovereign immunity to avoid paying the compensation awarded by the International Centre for Settlement of Investment Disputes (ICSID) to several renewable energy investors. The court agreed to initiate proceedings for the recognition and enforcement of seven of the 27 awards issued by the World Bank's arbitral tribunal, considering them to be final judgments.
This decision opened the door for the plaintiffs to request the seizure of Spanish assets and property as a precautionary measure to ensure the collection of compensation arising from the retroactive cut in renewable energy feed-in tariffs following the 2013 electricity reform.
In light of the Spanish government's refusal to pay these compensations-the Spanish defense argues that the Court of Justice of the European Union (CJEU) has limited the validity of arbitration between EU investors and member states, and that the European Commission must authorize such payments- the investors and opportunistic funds, led by Blasket Renewable Investments, requested the seizure of Spanish assets in several countries, including the United Kingdom, Belgium, and the Netherlands, to try to secure a debt that currently amounts to over 1.8 billion euros (2.3 billion, if litigation costs and legal and late payment interest are included).
In this context, the plaintiffs also filed a request in the United States for a court order to obtain various banking information and contracts (including invoices, receipts, transaction records, account statements, and bank transfer confirmations) from suppliers to the Spanish national team during the World Cup, which will take place from June 11 to July 19. Among the companies are Adidas, the hotel chains Vision Hospitality and Hilton, and the Baylor School in Chattanooga, Tennessee, whose sports facilities have been chosen as the team's base camp. In this way, the creditors are seeking any source of funding subject to seizure, such as public subsidies, FIFA funds, and television contracts.
Now, the asset search can be conducted on a broader scale after a Columbia court authorized the expansion of proceedings across the entire United States, and not just in Washington, according to a ruling dated April 10 reviewed by this newspaper. In that ruling, Judge Beryl A. Howell has agreed to allow the Blasket Fund to register in other U.S. districts the judgment validating one of the seven cases filed-specifically, the lawsuit brought by Watkins Holdings and other investors, whose damages were estimated at 77 million euros.
Sources at the Ministry of Ecological Transition emphasize that these asset-tracing procedures (known in U.S. legal jargon as the "discovery phase") "do not affect operations in Spain, much less those of the Spanish Football Federation, through the Spanish national team, as it is an entity independent of the State."
Freezes in a Dozen Districts
As things stand, the creditors' attorneys have already filed this case in a dozen districts to initiate potential seizure actions in those territories, according to the legal sources consulted. This includes states where the Spanish national team is scheduled to play matches or train during the soccer tournament, as well as any other location where Spain has economic or contractual activity. A potential asset subject to seizure or precautionary freezing, the same sources note, could be the means of transportation used by Spanish institutions for official travel to the United States, such as government aircraft.
In addition to the Spanish national team's movements, creditors have focused on an air defense contract signed with the U.S. company Raytheon, a subsidiary of RTX, for the supply of four Patriot missile defense systems, valued at 1.44 billion euros (1.7 billion dollars at the current exchange rate). Sources indicate that the plaintiffs' legal teams are exploring various avenues to try to block this transaction, announced in December 2025, or to seize any potential advance payments.
Furthermore, with the latest judicial backing in Columbia, opportunistic funds have issued new search warrants through entities such as the U.S. Federal Reserve (Fed) or The Clearing House Payments Company, the banking association and clearing and settlement firm for dollar payments among major financial institutions (owned by the country's largest commercial banks). Creditors are seeking to gain access to more information on accounts, deposits, transfers, and financial flows processed by the U.S. banking system since November 2021, when the first judicial acknowledgments of debt against Spain began to emerge in the United States.
Spain has appealed to the U.S. Supreme Court to take up this matter and strip the District Court of Columbia of jurisdiction over the seven renewable energy arbitration awards against Spain, which together amount to more than 600 million euros in recognized damages. Meanwhile, the Spanish government's legal team has sought to halt the seizure requests, but without success. Legal sources consulted indicate that they expect the Solicitor General-a figure similar to the Spanish state attorney-to issue his opinion on May 21 regarding whether the high court should hear this case. From there, the Supreme Court may request further briefs from Spain. If that happens, the ruling is expected to be handed down after the summer.

value.Title: Investigators from University of Tennessee Have Reported New Data on Sustainability Research (Spatial Optimization of the Sustainable Aviation Fuel Supply Chains From Forest Residues Via Fast Pyrolysis/hydrotreatment Considering Feedstock Ash ...)

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JH0-WHF3-S7SW-M25S-00000-00Investigators from University of Tennessee Have Reported New Data on Sustainability Research (Spatial Optimization of the Sustainable Aviation Fuel Supply Chains From Forest Residues Via Fast Pyrolysis/hydrotreatment Considering Feedstock Ash ...)2026-05-04T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:51ZLexisNexisSUSTAINABILITY RESEARCHInvestigators from University of Tennessee Have Reported New Data on Sustainability Research (Spatial Optimization of the Sustainable Aviation Fuel Supply Chains From Forest Residues Via Fast Pyrolysis/hydrotreatment Considering Feedstock Ash ...)


2026 MAY 04 (NewsRx) -- By a News Reporter-Staff News Editor at Ecology Daily News -- Investigators publish new report on Sustainability Research. According to news reporting originating in Knoxville, Tennessee, by NewsRx journalists, research stated, "Policymakers and the aviation industry are working to decarbonize commercial flights by replacing conventional jet fuel with sustainable aviation fuel (SAF). Logging residues have been identified as a valuable resource for SAF production."


Funders for this research include US Department of Agriculture, Agricultural and Food Research Initiative (AFRI), US Federal Aviation Administration (FAA) Office of Environment and Energy through ASCENT, FAA Center of Excellence for Alternative Jet Fuels and the Environment through FAA.
The news reporters obtained a quote from the research from the University of Tennessee, "However, the quality of the feedstock, particularly the ash content, can adversely affect bio-oil yield and SAF production using the fast pyrolysis/hydrotreatment process, and potentially its supply chain optimization. Previous research often assumes fixed biofuel yields and neglects the variability in feedstock quality when optimizing the supply chain. Thus, this study seeks to address this gap in the literature by employing a two-stage mixed-integer linear programming (MILP) model to investigate the influence of varying ash content in logging residues on the potential maximum supply quantity (MSQ) and net revenue (NR) of SAF production. Two scenarios were conducted in the Southeastern United States (US): one assuming constant ash content and the other accounting for heterogeneous ash content in logging residues. Results indicate that ignoring ash variability in the feedstock could lead to overestimation of SAF MSQ and NR by 14.15 % and 18.27 %, respectively, in the study area. Additionally, higher ash content leads to lower bio-oil yields, resulting in fewer refineries and reduced capacity. The study emphasizes the need for best management practices to mitigate soil contamination during feedstock processing and improve the resilience of the biomass-based SAF supply chain."
According to the news reporters, the research concluded: "Furthermore, it is crucial to effectively manage the mechanisms of mineral uptake and their integration into the structure of lignocellulosic materials."
This research has been peer-reviewed.
For more information on this research see: Spatial Optimization of the Sustainable Aviation Fuel Supply Chains From Forest Residues Via Fast Pyrolysis/hydrotreatment Considering Feedstock Ash Content Variability. Biomass & Bioenergy, 2026;208. Biomass & Bioenergy can be contacted at: Pergamon-elsevier Science Ltd, The Boulevard, Langford Lane, Kidlington, Oxford OX5 1GB, England. (Elsevier - www.elsevier.com; Biomass & Bioenergy - www.journals.elsevier.com/biomass-and-bioenergy/)
Our news correspondents report that additional information may be obtained by contacting T. Edward Yu, University of Tennessee, Dept. of Agricultural and Resource Economics, Knoxville, TN 37996, United States. Additional authors for this research include Pengzhen Li, Burton C. English, Nicole Labbe, Nourredine Abdoulmoumine, Manuel Garcia-Perez and Kevin P. Hoyt.
The direct object identifier (DOI) for that additional information is: https://doi.org/10.1016/j.biombioe.2025.108793. This DOI is a link to an online electronic document that is either free or for purchase, and can be your direct source for a journal article and its citation.
Keywords for this news article include: Knoxville, Tennessee, United States, North and Central America, Aviation, Sustainability Research, University of Tennessee.
Our reports deliver fact-based news of research and discoveries from around the world. Copyright 2026, NewsRx LLC

value.Title: M . A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM DPR CONSTRUCTION AND ITS PEERS

Document.Content:
urn:contentItem:6JGC-DM23-S9SW-31B3-00000-00M. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM DPR CONSTRUCTION AND ITS PEERS2026-05-01T00:00:00Z2026-05-28T16:11:52ZLexisNexisSTOCKM. A. MORTENSON COMPANIES INC. - NEWS AND TRENDS FROM DPR CONSTRUCTION AND ITS PEERS

DASHBOARD: M A MORTENSON

PermID5001069053Websitehttp://www.mortenson.comIndustryConstruction

ACTIVITIES:

M. A. Mortenson Companies Inc., founded in 1954 by M.A. Mortenson Sr., is a top-20 U.S. builder headquartered in Minneapolis, Minnesota. With 5,000 employees across 12 offices, it delivers construction, development, and energy services, generating $5.7 billion in 2023 revenue. Mortenson specializes in healthcare, renewable energy, and data centers, with projects like Allianz Field. Its integrated services emphasize sustainability and innovation, using advanced technologies like digital modeling. Competing with Gilbane, Mortensons commitment to safety and diversity, along with its North American operations, ensures high-performing project delivery.

+ M A MORTENSON [1 News That Matters]

+ M A MORTENSON WINS $610M BIOSECURITY CONSTRUCTION PROJECT TARGETING NEW WORLD SCREWWORM

M A Mortenson Company, a Minneapolis-based construction firm, has been awarded a $610 million contract by the U.S. Army Corps of Engineers to build the Domestic New World Screwworm Sterile Insect Production Facility at Moore Airbase in Edinburg, Texas. The contract is part of a broader effort to curb the spread of New World screwworms in the United States. On March 9, 2026, officials warned of the threat if the screwworm migrates north from Mexico, with the USDA signaling urgency as cases are reported in Tamaulipas near the Texas border. As of March 4, 2026, there were 989 active cases nationwide, including 19 in Tamaulipas, up from 11 weeks earlier, illustrating the escalating risk to livestock. Officials said the project will help maintain a steady supply of sterilized flies used to disrupt the screwworm life cycle, a key component of border protection and the broader biosecurity strategy, with Mortenson coordinating construction alongside USDA efforts.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ JASON PAGE APPOINTED HEAD OF DPR CONSTRUCTION'S NASHVILLE SPECIAL SERVICES GROUP

The Special Services Group handles a broad range of work, from targeted improvements like parking garage stair installations to expansive interior buildouts for new office and entertainment spaces, and Page's leadership is expected to expand the group's capabilities to meet evolving client and stakeholder needs in Nashville. Beyond project leadership, Page mentors and coaches teams, sharing best practices for quick-turn, high-impact work and fostering a culture of collaboration and innovation as DPR expands its Nashville footprint.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION SECURES GREAT PLACE TO WORK STATUS AS STAFF REPORT HIGH ENGAGEMENT

DPR Construction has secured Great Place To Work Certification, confirming that 97 percent of its employees say it is a great place to work, a figure about 40 points higher than the national average. The award, presented on 31st March 2026, signals a culture rooted in pride, belonging and opportunity across the firm’s projects.CEO George Pfeffer noted the certification reflects sentiments across the organization. "Being Certified(TradeMark) as a great place to work is especially meaningful because it comes directly from our people, who bring our culture to life," he said. The company emphasizes trust, support, and growth to help employees perform at their best.DPR’s operating model empowers staff to make decisions and share information openly, encouraging initiative and cross functional collaboration on complex tasks, new roles, and ideas beyond job descriptions. A strong emphasis on learning and development supports clear career paths aligned with individual aspirations.Great Place To Work® research aligns with these findings, highlighting a sense of pride and belonging among workers. The certification has helped DPR strengthen its reputation as an employer of choice, while external accolades from U.S. News & World Report and Newsweek have recognized DPR in more than 15 U.S. cities over the last year.Founded in 1990, DPR has evolved into a multi billion dollar contractor known for complex and sustainable projects across sectors. Its innovation posture—through virtual design and construction and self performing craft—helps explain its roughly 11,000 person workforce worldwide. More information about DPR’s culture and career opportunities is available on its official website.

+ DPR CONSTRUCTION GUIDES SOUTH FLORIDA'S GROWTH THROUGH COLLABORATION AND TECH

On the 28th of March 2026, during an industry roundtable hosted in partnership with the South Florida Business Journal, leaders from development, construction, and government agencies explored how South Florida’s skyline is evolving and the role DPR Construction is playing in guiding that change.Collaboration Reshaping Project Delivery: Participants stressed that cross sector partnerships have shifted from optional to essential for navigating modern urban development. By aligning stakeholders early, projects gain measurable improvements in efficiency, safety, and quality. In a market with limited land, rising costs, and increasing regulatory demands, integrated approaches are critical to delivering long term community value rather than just buildings.Innovation and Technology Driving Efficiency: DPR Construction and its partners are leveraging prefabrication, modular construction, and emerging technologies like artificial intelligence to streamline workflows, reduce waste, and shorten lead times. Digital tools and data driven decision making support better forecasting and coordination across complex, multi phase programs, while improving site safety and accuracy.Addressing Workforce and Growth Challenges: South Florida’s strong population and economy are translating into a growing need for skilled labor. Industry leaders highlighted partnerships with schools and training programs designed to attract, develop, and retain talent within the construction sector, ensuring a steady pipeline as investment accelerates.Building for Long-Term Resilience: Finally, the discussion centered on sustainable building practices, energy efficiency, and climate resilient infrastructure. Developers and contractors aim to design communities that endure large scale change, balancing speed, cost, and sustainability. DPR Construction is positioned at the forefront of this evolution, setting benchmarks for large scale urban development in high demand markets.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ TURNER CONSTRUCTION [3 News That Matters]

+ TURNER CELEBRATES TOPPING OUT OF 72-MW ALIGNED IAD-06 DATA CENTER IN FREDERICK

Turner Construction Company marked the topping out of the Aligned IAD-06 Data Center in Frederick, Maryland, signifying the completion of the structure for the 72-megawatt facility. The milestone was achieved on the 28th of April 2026, less than five months after ground had been broken. To date, the project has logged more than 80,000 craft hours, with roughly 345 skilled workers contributing to progress. The 450,000-square-foot center sits on a 75-acre campus and features a two-story structural steel design, an insulated precast exterior system, and a high-efficiency hybrid cooling solution that combines liquid and air-cooled technologies to optimize performance. Turner's leadership emphasized that topping out represents more than erecting the frame, underscoring the strength of the partnership with Aligned and the team’s commitment to safe, efficient delivery. As the project moves into interior fit-out and operational readiness, stakeholders will watch closely, anticipating the facility’s role in supporting the region’s digital infrastructure.

+ GEISINGER WYOMING VALLEY EXPANSION MOVES TO VERTICAL CONSTRUCTION AS TURNER BREAKS GROUND

WILKES-BARRE, PA — Turner Construction has advanced to vertical construction on Geisinger Wyoming Valley Medical Center's $900 million expansion, moving forward after years of planning and foundation work. Steel now rises on an 11-story, 600,000-square-foot patient tower that will lift beds from 356 to 500 and expand operating rooms from 12 to 18. Officials say the addition will enlarge the emergency department and ensure all patient rooms are private, with completion expected in the first quarter of 2028. The date of this announcement is 28th April 2026.David Kaminski, Turner’s Vice President and Senior General Manager, described the milestone as a key step toward broader health care access for families in northeastern Pennsylvania and a chance to partner with Geisinger on a project that strengthens the community for decades. The project is projected to create as many as 3,800 construction jobs and generate about $1 billion in local economic impact. Onsite employment is expected to rise from 150 workers to as many as 450 during peak activity, with Geisinger planning to hire an additional 400 staff after opening.Geisinger Wyoming Valley's campus opened in 1981 and has since expanded to meet rising demand for medical services. The new tower will bring advanced capabilities comparable to those typically found in major academic medical centers serving large metropolitan areas.

+ JMA VENTURES NAMES TURNER CONSTRUCTION LEAD ON WESTCOURT ORLANDO DEVELOPMENT

Turner Construction has been named the general contractor for the Westcourt Orlando mixed-use project, a development planned for West Church Street in downtown Orlando. The announcement was made by developers on the 22nd of April, 2026, marking a milestone in the project’s progression. The decision to partner with Turner stems from a collaborative approach taken by JMA Ventures. Todd Chapman, president of JMA Ventures, said that Turner’s team demonstrated exceptional capabilities in prior complex builds, including coordinating infrastructure for rocket launches on Florida’s Space Coast. He recalled an early meeting in which he told the team, "If you guys can send things into space, I’m pretty sure you can work with us." Turner has been involved in the Westcourt project since 2022, handling pre-construction with JMA Ventures and Machete Group. Early site activities such as utility mapping and surveying are underway to ensure a smooth transition into the next phases. The Westcourt project is valued at approximately $500 million and will include a hotel, residential units, office space, retail, and a live entertainment venue adjacent to the Kia Center, with peak on-site employment around 800 workers.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ JE DUNN CONSTRUCTION GROUP [3 News That Matters]

+ JE DUNN LAUNCHES FORM OFF-SITE SOLUTIONS TO ACCELERATE CONSTRUCTION MANUFACTURING

JE Dunn Construction has launched Form Off-Site Solutions, its off-site manufacturing initiative focused on Design for Manufacturing (DfM) for complex construction components. The program, announced with a launch date of 6th April 2026, builds on the company’s century-long Kansas City heritage while extending capabilities nationwide.Form Off-Site Solutions structures its offerings into four product segments: metal assemblies, wood assemblies, building skin assemblies, and multi-trade assemblies, designed to meet the varied needs of owners, general contractors, trade partners, and original equipment manufacturers.The end-to-end service model covers estimating, design, manufacturing engineering, project management, and production, all aimed at improving installation efficiency, safety, and quality, while providing greater cost certainty and helping shorten project timelines amid labor shortages.Off-Site Manufacturing Director Nick Effenheim emphasizes collaboration: "Anything Form designs, our people can build. Our ability to collaborate with project teams and focus on getting their ideas, concepts, and design into a manufactured environment is something that sets us apart."JE Dunn President and CEO Gordon Lansford says the Form launch marks a major step in the firm’s ongoing push to drive innovation for clients as Form’s production facilities in Kansas City and Nashville position the unit to serve a national client base.

+ STEEL BEAM PLACED AS NORTHLAND CENTER REACHES TOPPING-OUT MILESTONE

KANSAS CITY, Mo. — The topping-out of the Northland Workforce Development Center was celebrated on 19th March 2026, as JE Dunn Construction placed the final structural steel beam for the 145,000-square-foot, two-story facility.Before the beam was hoisted into place, a beam-signing ceremony brought together the Platte County R-3 School District, partner districts, project stakeholders, and community members who signed the beam bearing project and partner logos. Brad Hokanson, Project Executive at JE Dunn Construction, said, 'This project is about creating opportunities and pathways for the next generation of skilled professionals in our region.'The center is being built to house programs in skilled trades, manufacturing, health sciences, and human services, expanding capacity for Northland Career Center and partner institutions while serving both high school students and adult learners seeking credentials. Planned spaces include flexible labs, classrooms, hands-on training environments, and technology-rich areas that reflect real-world workplaces.Located in the Northland region of Kansas City, the project is progressing on schedule, with substantial completion targeted in 2027 and a summer opening expected after the milestone. Executive Director Brian Noller of Platte County School District noted that state, local government, business, investor, and community foundation support have enabled a long-term investment in students and the region.JE Dunn is serving as construction manager, working in partnership with the school district and Clark & Enersen, with a focus on safety, schedule adherence, and coordination while maintaining open communication with stakeholders and minimizing disruption to nearby facilities.

+ JE DUNN REINSTATES CHRIS CHELETTE AS SENIOR VP, HEALTHCARE, TO EXPAND REGIONAL PRESENCE

JE Dunn Construction has announced the return of Chris Chelette, who will serve as the Senior Vice President – Healthcare. Chelette's reappointment comes after he spent nine years as the Senior Vice President of Facilities Services at Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This announcement was made on 13th June 2025. During his previous 17-year tenure with JE Dunn, Chelette played a crucial role in expanding the company's healthcare business unit. His extensive experience includes overseeing numerous high-profile projects across the United States, particularly within the non-profit and for-profit healthcare sectors. His return to JE Dunn is seen as a strategic move to enhance the company's healthcare presence in the southeastern region. In his new role, Chelette will be responsible for providing strategic leadership and operational oversight for healthcare construction projects throughout the southeast. Jeff Fuller, President of Healthcare at JE Dunn, expressed enthusiasm about Chelette's return, noting his visionary leadership and impactful experience in the healthcare field. Fuller emphasized that Chelette brings a national perspective and a proven track record that will benefit JE Dunn's future endeavors in healthcare construction. Throughout his career, Chelette has been instrumental in managing significant projects, such as the development of the North Druid Hills campus for Children’s Healthcare of Atlanta. This project, which totaled over $2 billion, featured the Center for Advanced Pediatrics, along with a state-of-the-art 446-bed replacement hospital that opened last year. Such accomplishments highlight Chelette's capability to manage complex healthcare construction initiatives effectively. As JE Dunn continues to invest in its healthcare construction division, Chelette's return is expected to drive growth and innovation in the company's project portfolio. The organization aims to leverage his expertise to enhance its service offerings and solidify its position as a leader in the healthcare construction industry.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ GILBANE [3 News That Matters]

+ ANDREAS VLAHAKIS APPOINTED TO LEAD CAPITAL MARKETS AT GILBANE DEVELOPMENT

As of 28th April 2026, Gilbane Development has named Andreas Vlahakis as head of capital markets, to operate from the firm's New York City office. He brings extensive experience in real estate capital markets across asset classes, with a focus on ground-up multifamily and student housing investments.In this role, Vlahakis will refine and execute Gilbane's funding strategy, report to James Patchett, president and CEO, and help shape the capital strategy, drive investment decisions, and nurture relationships with funding and equity partners. He will also lead transaction structuring and negotiations to position Gilbane to capitalize on evolving market opportunities.Previously, Vlahakis served as managing director for capital markets at Thor Equities, leading equity-raising efforts for a range of projects and partnerships. His prior role as senior vice president and chief of staff at Thor Equities underscores his broad financial leadership as the firm pursues growth.Gilbane Development says the appointment aligns with its commitment to quality and innovation and positions the firm for growth by strengthening its capital discipline and investment program.Investors will likely be watching how Vlahakis's background translates into stronger capital relationships and execution in Gilbane's future real estate deals.

+ WKU FORMS GILBANE ALLIANCE TO DEVELOP NEW RESIDENCE HALL AND UPGRADES

The Western Kentucky University Student Government Association announced the formal approval of WKU's public-private partnership with Gilbane, designed to expand housing and campus facilities. The agreement was approved by WKU's Board of Regents on 11th December 2025, with Gilbane tasked to design and build a new residence hall to replace Douglas Keen and Hugh Poland Halls. With the Kentucky Legislature's approval on 6th April 2026, construction is authorized to commence. SGA Senator Jakob Barker confirmed that the partnership with Gilbane is fully authorized and stated, "WKU is now in the clear to start building the new residence halls." Beyond housing, the SGA advanced several measures aimed at improving student resources. Resolution 7-26-S backs installing a crosswalk at a key intersection near housing and urges collaboration with the Kentucky Department of Transportation to address student safety. Bill 17-26-S creates the Textbooks for Toppers program, which will provide $50 reimbursements for required course materials. Additional bills will supply essential academic items such as Scantrons and lab goggles to reduce out-of-pocket costs for students. Taken together, the developments reflect WKU's broader strategy to enhance the student experience through strategic housing development and targeted support for student resources.

+ PHASE II LEASING OPENS FOR GILBANE’S $135M BARNABY & 7TH DEVELOPMENT IN DC

Gilbane Development has moved Phase II of the Barnaby & 7th project into leasing, adding 229 units in four new buildings: The Stoneview, The Solomon, The Marte, and The Lewis in Southeast Washington, D.C. As of 3rd April 2026, Phase II is scheduled for completion by summer 2026 and provides a mix of studio to three-bedroom units designed for households earning 50 percent and 80 percent of AMI. Twenty percent of the apartments will be Permanent Supportive Housing units, managed by the District's Department of Homeless Services.Phase I, The Upland, was finished in Spring 2025 and contains 169 apartments, including 34 PSH units. Income limits for Phase I range from 30 to 50 percent of AMI.The third phase is planned to add roughly 100 more rental units in a single building, bringing the project total to about 498 units upon completion.During Phase II construction, a new pedestrian walkway was built to connect all six buildings within the larger development. The District’s Housing Finance Agency estimates Phase II cost at about $135 million, illustrating Gilbane's commitment to affordable housing and enhanced community connectivity in Washington, D.C.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++

+ CLARK CONSTRUCTION GROUP, LLC [3 News That Matters]

+ RENFREWSHIRE COUNCIL AWARDS MULTI-TRADES FRAMEWORK CONTRACT TO CLARK CONTRACTS FOR DOMESTIC HOUSING WORKS

Clark Contracts has been appointed to Renfrewshire Council's multi-trades contractors framework to improve delivery of repair, maintenance and refurbishment services for domestic properties across the authority. The appointment was announced on 11th February 2026 and the framework runs through 2027.The framework covers a broad range of multi-trade works, enabling the council to manage both responsive and planned maintenance more effectively across its housing stock and ensuring residents benefit from a faster, more reliable service.Clark Contracts is a well-established public-sector supplier, with current agreements including Scotland Excel’s New Build Residential Framework and NHS National Services Scotland’s Minor Works Framework. The company also participates in the Crown Commercial Service’s Construction Framework and several Scottish Procurement Alliance frameworks.Gordon Cunningham, managing director at Clark Contracts, said the appointment builds on a track record of more than £200 million in refurbishment projects over the past three years and demonstrates the firm’s commitment to serving local communities.The deal supports Clark Contracts’ growing public-sector footprint as it works with councils and institutions such as the University of Glasgow and Glasgow City Council to deliver essential housing services.

+ CDC APPROVES AMENDMENT FOR ₱4-B THE VILLAGES DEVELOPMENT, PKR JOINS CLARK GROWTH DRIVE

Clark Freeport is set to host a ₱4-billion mixed-use residential and commercial development in The Villages after Clark Development Corp. approved an amendment to PKR Corp.'s lease on December 13, 2025. The revised lease provides PKR with a 30-month window to complete construction and includes safeguards aimed at ensuring on-time delivery. Atty. Agnes VST Devanadera, CDC president and CEO, noted that the amendment reinforces investor confidence and the efficient execution of projects, while keeping The Villages as a community hub where residents can live and participate. PKR Corp. president Daekwon Jung thanked CDC for the partnership and said the company will deliver the Clark project, with PKR operating under Kyeryong Construction Industrial Co., Ltd., a South Korean firm known for safety, technology, and workplace practices. The initiative fits CDC's broader push to accelerate investments, promote well-planned communities, and establish a business-friendly environment that appeals to both global and local investors.

+ VPM, CLARK CONSTRUCTION UNVEIL NEW HEADQUARTERS AIMED AT FUTURE BUILDERS

VPM welcomed students from the Metropolitan Business League to its new headquarters, which Clark Construction is building. The event, part of a 'Youth in Construction' initiative, took place on 19th November 2025 and gave Richmond Technical Center students an opportunity to explore trades across the construction sector. During the tour, attendees were introduced to carpentry, electrical work, HVAC, and plumbing. The program also highlighted Baker Concrete's apprenticeship program, illustrating the variety of career paths available. This practical experience enabled students to picture themselves working on future projects of a similar scale. VPM broke ground on the new headquarters in August 2024, with an expected completion in 2026. The Monroe Ward relocation is meant to centralize VPM’s news and content production, increasing public access and engagement. By deepening its footprint in Richmond, VPM seeks to enhance local coverage of news and events. Beyond trades, the students interacted with VPM's broadcasting capabilities, recording clips in the mobile sound booth to gain a firsthand sense of the organization's daily operations that deliver news to millions via TV and radio channels. Community support is essential for VPM, helping sustain local news, educational programming, and inspiring content. As VPM advances its work with Clark Construction, the partnership demonstrates how such initiatives can enrich local education and industry awareness.

++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++++
